programme.title: Killer Storms and Cruel Winters: The History of Extreme Weather - Timewatch
programme.desc: Documentary following solar scientist Dr Lucie Green as she reveals how some of the UK's worst and most dramatic weather disasters occurred many years ago, despite the popular belief that the brunt of climate change has been felt in the 21st century. Considering how many people today are pre-warned about potential catastrophes due to innovations in weather prediction, the presenter learns how an 18th-century storm surge once led to a the deaths of a thousand workers in Somerset fields, a hurricane drowned a fifth of Britain's naval officers, and several brutal winters threatened to completely shut down most services in the country

programme.title: Winterwatch 1963: The Big Freeze
programme.desc: Chris Packham introduces a documentary from the BBC archive that takes a look at the worst British winter of the 20th century. He also explores how this severe weather 50 years ago affected the nation's wildlife and how it would cope if another equally bad winter occurred

programme.title: Beethoven's Fifth at the Proms
programme.desc: Petroc Trelawny and Georgia Mann present a specially curated night of musical gems from the Royal Albert Hall featuring the BBC Symphony Orchestra and conductors Marin Alsop and Elim Chan. Alongside a performance of one of the best-known of all symphonies is Mussorgsky's A Night on a Bare Mountain and Richard Strauss's Don Juan

programme.title: A303: Highway to the Sun
programme.desc: Tom Fort travels along the 92-mile-long A303 from the Hampshire town of Basingstoke to Honiton in Devon. Originally designed in the 1960s, the route has been the subject of many proposed upgrades - several of which were strongly opposed due to their potential impact on sites of historic and natural significance, including Stonehenge. The programme charts the highway's turbulent history, as well as examining the development of the region over the past 5,000 years

programme.title: Hans Zimmer: Hollywood Rebel
programme.desc: Profile of the Oscar-winning composer responsible for some of the best-loved film and TV scores of the past few decades. Contributors include directors Christopher Nolan, Denis Villeneuve, and Steve McQueen. In this portrait, Zimmer reveals the musical secrets of his craft - how he goes about terrifying, moving and raising people's spirits through his work

programme.title: Beethoven's Fifth at the Proms
programme.desc: Petroc Trelawny and Georgia Mann present a specially curated night of musical gems from the Royal Albert Hall featuring the BBC Symphony Orchestra and conductors Marin Alsop and Elim Chan. Alongside a performance of one of the best-known of all symphonies is Mussorgsky's A Night on a Bare Mountain and Richard Strauss's Don Juan

programme.title: Killer Storms and Cruel Winters: The History of Extreme Weather - Timewatch
programme.desc: Documentary following solar scientist Dr Lucie Green as she reveals how some of the UK's worst and most dramatic weather disasters occurred many years ago, despite the popular belief that the brunt of climate change has been felt in the 21st century. Considering how many people today are pre-warned about potential catastrophes due to innovations in weather prediction, the presenter learns how an 18th-century storm surge once led to a the deaths of a thousand workers in Somerset fields, a hurricane drowned a fifth of Britain's naval officers, and several brutal winters threatened to completely shut down most services in the country

programme.title: A303: Highway to the Sun
programme.desc: Tom Fort travels along the 92-mile-long A303 from the Hampshire town of Basingstoke to Honiton in Devon. Originally designed in the 1960s, the route has been the subject of many proposed upgrades - several of which were strongly opposed due to their potential impact on sites of historic and natural significance, including Stonehenge. The programme charts the highway's turbulent history, as well as examining the development of the region over the past 5,000 years

programme.title: Atlantic: The Wildest Ocean on Earth
programme.desc: Cillian Murphy narrates a documentary examining the lives of animals and people pitting themselves against the world's wildest ocean, unveiling the natural forces that underpin life in the Atlantic, from the world's most powerful currents, ferocious hurricanes and huge chains of undersea volcanoes. The first episode looks at the influence of the Gulf Stream, with whales, giant turtles and jellyfish riding the huge current that becomes a migration super-highway and helps warm northern Europe, while fishermen battle for survival in mountainous seas as they try to exploit its natural fertility

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Greece
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke focuses on the reasons behind the revolution that gave birth to classical Greek art. He travels in Sicily to the Valley of the Temples in Agrigento before heading to the island of Mozia to see the Charioteer, which was unearthed in 1979. He also marvels at the athletic bodies of the Riace bronzes - warriors dragged from the seabed - as he asks how the Greeks became so good so quickly

programme.title: Private Life of a Masterpiece
programme.desc: James McNeill Whistler's Portrait of the Artist's Mother is one of the most satirised paintings of all time. Yet when his sombre masterpiece was first unveiled, it was seen as a radical departure from the sentimental images beloved of the Victorians, and which Whistler loathed. First broadcast in 2004

programme.title: One Hundred Great Paintings
programme.desc: Milton Brown presents a closer look at James Whistler's painting Nocturne in Black and Gold: The Falling Rocket at the Institute of Art in Detroit

programme.title: Becoming Frida Kahlo
programme.desc: Documentary charting the life and career of the Mexican artist, beginning with her early years when a young Frida grew up in a time of revolution. The programme looks at her rebellious schooldays and a fateful encounter with Diego Rivera, then one of the world's most famous artists and the man who would change her life

programme.title: Stolen: Catching the Art Thieves
programme.desc: A look at the police investigation into the theft of Edvard Munch paintings The Scream and Madonna from Oslo's Munch Museum in June 2004, which revealed the lengths criminals will go to gain a get-out-of-jail card for murder

programme.title: Atlantic: The Wildest Ocean on Earth
programme.desc: Cillian Murphy narrates a documentary examining the lives of animals and people pitting themselves against the world's wildest ocean, unveiling the natural forces that underpin life in the Atlantic, from the world's most powerful currents, ferocious hurricanes and huge chains of undersea volcanoes. The first episode looks at the influence of the Gulf Stream, with whales, giant turtles and jellyfish riding the huge current that becomes a migration super-highway and helps warm northern Europe, while fishermen battle for survival in mountainous seas as they try to exploit its natural fertility

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Greece
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke focuses on the reasons behind the revolution that gave birth to classical Greek art. He travels in Sicily to the Valley of the Temples in Agrigento before heading to the island of Mozia to see the Charioteer, which was unearthed in 1979. He also marvels at the athletic bodies of the Riace bronzes - warriors dragged from the seabed - as he asks how the Greeks became so good so quickly

programme.title: Stolen: Catching the Art Thieves
programme.desc: A look at the police investigation into the theft of Edvard Munch paintings The Scream and Madonna from Oslo's Munch Museum in June 2004, which revealed the lengths criminals will go to gain a get-out-of-jail card for murder

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's Scottish travels bring him to Glasgow's Hampden Park Stadium, the home of Scottish football, where he admires the oldest football trophy in the world and hears about the birth of the 'passing game'. He also learns about the development of the M8 motorway in the 1960s and visits Kelvingrove Art Gallery, set up by war artist Joan Eardley amid the tenements of Townhead. He finishes this leg in the modernist new town of Cumbernauld, hearing about the utopian ideals that shaped its design and architecture

programme.title: Walking with Kate Garraway
programme.desc: The broadcaster walks through the Cotswolds and describes the joy and solace that nature brings as she takes in the inspiring views of Gloucestershire. From the panoramic viewpoint of Painswick Beacon, Kate descends through beech woods to the village of Upton St Leonards, soaking up the sounds of nature as she goes

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Richard finds time weighing heavily on his hands, so Hyacinth decides to provide him with a hobby - a move that backfires on her in spectacular fashion. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor and Margaret set off for a break by the seaside - but the discovery of a dead gull in their boarding-house bathroom sets the scene for what turns out to be a weekend of sheer hell. Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie star, with a guest appearance by Stephen Lewis of On the Buses fame

programme.title: She-Wolves: England's Early Queens
programme.desc: Helen Castor explores the role of queens in medieval and Tudor England, analysing how they evolved from being the wives of kings to powerful figures in their own right - but faced great struggles to impose their authority in a male-dominated society. She begins by recalling the life of Matilda, the daughter of Henry I, who waged war against her cousin Stephen in the mid-12th-century in a bid to be recognised as her father's rightful successor. The historian also charts the turbulent life of Eleanor of Aquitaine, the wife of Henry II, who played a major role in governing England during the latter half of the 12th century

programme.title: Castles: Britain's Fortified History
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis charts the stories of Britain's castles and their roles in the country's history, art and literature. He begins by looking at the structure's first appearance during Norman rule in 1066, and then at the siege of Kenilworth Castle 200 years later, revealing how it was initially used as an instrument of invasion, but soon became a weapon with which unruly barons challenged the crown. He also visits Tintagel Castle in Cornwall, the place where King Arthur is said to have been conceived

programme.title: Corridors of Power: Should America Police the World?
programme.desc: Examining the results of the White House's decision not to act in response to the 1994 genocide in Rwanda , which still haunts the Washington decision-makers to this day. Featuring descriptions of the events from American diplomats on the ground and in Washington as the tragedy unfolded

programme.title: Corridors of Power: Should America Police the World?
programme.desc: Examining how Muslim separatists in the Kosovo region rekindled old conflicts that led President Clinton's White House to consider Serbian President Milosevic's regime a threat to their own civilians. Plus, a look at how the Russian President Boris Yeltsin opposed Western intervention against their old allies in the Balkans, as well as when he was forced to resign, leaving a young Vladimir Putin to take his place

programme.title: She-Wolves: England's Early Queens
programme.desc: Helen Castor explores the role of queens in medieval and Tudor England, analysing how they evolved from being the wives of kings to powerful figures in their own right - but faced great struggles to impose their authority in a male-dominated society. She begins by recalling the life of Matilda, the daughter of Henry I, who waged war against her cousin Stephen in the mid-12th-century in a bid to be recognised as her father's rightful successor. The historian also charts the turbulent life of Eleanor of Aquitaine, the wife of Henry II, who played a major role in governing England during the latter half of the 12th century

From the panoramic viewpoint of Painswick Beacon, Kate descends through beech woods to the village of Upton St Leonards, soaking up the sounds of nature as she goes

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

He also learns about the development of the M8 motorway in the 1960s and visits Kelvingrove Art Gallery, set up by war artist Joan Eardley amid the tenements of Townhead. He finishes this leg in the modernist new town of Cumbernauld, hearing about the utopian ideals that shaped its design and architecture

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo joins a women's running club for a warm-up session in Glasgow and then heads to Edinburgh, where he investigates a puzzling incident from 1979 when a forestry worker claimed to have been attacked by an alien spaceship

programme.title: Walking with Nick Grimshaw
programme.desc: The presenter and DJ escapes for a revitalising walk along the Northumberland coast and reflects on his career, growing up and plans for starting a family. Nick explores the streets of Warkworth, Warkworth Hermitage, a retreat carved from a sandstone cliff, and Amble pier

programme.title: The Mary Rose: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Historian Dan Snow explores the discovery, excavation and raising of the ship, finding out what the latest research has revealed about the vessel and her crew. The presenter also investigates how the project helped create modern underwater archaeology, and examines the techniques, challenges and successes of the divers and archaeologists involved

programme.title: The Magic of Mushrooms
programme.desc: Richard Fortey examines the world of mushrooms in a specially built laboratory with the help of mycologist Patrick Hickey. Together, they reveal the ability of fungi to break down waste and sustain new plant life, keeping the planet alive. Richard also travels across Britain and further afield to reveal their mysterious life cycles and how they have the potential to open new frontiers in science, medicine and technology

programme.title: Edna, the Inebriate Woman - Play for Today
programme.desc: A down-and-out woman wanders through life in an alcoholic haze without a job, a home or any money. Jeremy Sandford's 1971 drama, starring Patricia Hayes and Barbara Jefford. First broadcast October 21, 1971

programme.title: Private Life of a Masterpiece
programme.desc: James McNeill Whistler's Portrait of the Artist's Mother is one of the most satirised paintings of all time. Yet when his sombre masterpiece was first unveiled, it was seen as a radical departure from the sentimental images beloved of the Victorians, and which Whistler loathed. First broadcast in 2004

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo joins a women's running club for a warm-up session in Glasgow and then heads to Edinburgh, where he investigates a puzzling incident from 1979 when a forestry worker claimed to have been attacked by an alien spaceship

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: At the former Midlothian mining village of Newtongrange, Michael Portillo meets the son of a miner whose name loomed large in the disputes of the 1970s and 1980s - vice-president of the National Union of Mineworkers, 'Red' Mick McGahey. On the outskirts of Edinburgh, at a recording studio in South Gyle, Michael is transported back to the wild world of 1970s pop with Bay City Rollers guitarist, Stuart 'Woody' Wood, and hears how the teenage boyband topped the charts

programme.title: Walking with Shappi Khorsandi
programme.desc: Comedian and author Shappi Khorsandi walks through the Peak District and along the Monsal Trail. From the hills above Tideswell, Shappi follows tracks and footpaths along Tideswell Dale and Miller's Dale. Shappi talks about relationships, motherhood and mental health, as well as sharing her outlook on life and love

programme.title: Jesus Christ Superstar
programme.desc: Musical following the last days in the life of Jesus Christ, seen from the perspective of the disciple Judas, who is gradually tempted into betraying his friend and sending him to his death. Based on the Tim Rice and Andrew Lloyd Webber stage show and starring Ted Neeley, Carl Anderson, Yvonne Elliman and Barry Dennan

programme.title: Norman Jewison: Hollywood Veteran - HardTalk
programme.desc: Tim Sebastian talks to Hollywood director and producer Norman Jewison. His acclaimed films include In the Heat of the Night, 1967, Fiddler on the Roof, 1971 and Moonstruck, 1987, which starred American singer and actress Cher

programme.title: In the Heat of the Night
programme.desc: When a wealthy industrialist is murdered in Mississippi, the bigoted local sheriff is quick to accuse a black newcomer to town - only to learn his suspect is a highly respected homicide detective from Philadelphia. However, the duo are forced to put aside their mutual loathing and team up to track down the killer. Oscar-winning drama, starring Sidney Poitier, Rod Steiger and Warren Oates

programme.title: Rod Steiger: Scene by Scene
programme.desc: Mark Cousins interviews Rod Steiger, star of numerous classics including On the Waterfront, Dr Zhivago, Oklahoma, The Pawnbroker and In the Heat of the Night. Never a conventional leading man, Steiger nevertheless changed the direction of movies and opened the way for the distinctive performances of Robert de Niro, who referred to him as the most important actor of his generation

programme.title: Castles: Britain's Fortified History
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis charts the stories of Britain's castles and their roles in the country's history, art and literature. He begins by looking at the structure's first appearance during Norman rule in 1066, and then at the siege of Kenilworth Castle 200 years later, revealing how it was initially used as an instrument of invasion, but soon became a weapon with which unruly barons challenged the crown. He also visits Tintagel Castle in Cornwall, the place where King Arthur is said to have been conceived

programme.title: TOTP: 2000
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on February 18, 2000. Featuring Gabrielle, Daphne & Celeste, Hepburn, Joey Negro and Taka Boom, Eiffel 65, Ian Brown, DJ Luck & MC Neat and Oasis

programme.title: TOTP: 1982
programme.desc: Simon Bates presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on July 22, 1982. Featuring The Belle Stars, Madness, Bananarama, The Brat, Trio, Junior, The Stranglers, Dollar and Irene Cara

programme.title: TOTP: 1986
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Peter Powell present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on July 23, 1986. Featuring Hollywood Beyond, Sinitta, Audrey Hall, Robert Palmer, Haywoode and Madonna

programme.title: Prog Rock at the BBC
programme.desc: Footage from the BBC studios of some of Britain's most popular progressive rock bands dating back to the early 1970s. Including performances by Emerson, Lake and Palmer, Yes, Genesis, Caravan, Gentle Giant, Family and Atomic Rooster

programme.title: Genesis: Old Grey Whistle Test
programme.desc: Annie Nightingale introduces Genesis live at the Lyceum Ballroom, London, on May 7 1980, filmed during their tour for the album Duke. Songs include Behind the Lines, Duchess, Guide Vocal, In the Cage and Dance on a Volcano

programme.title: Jethro Tull: The Lively Arts
programme.desc: Documentary takin a look at the many-sided talents of the successful British rock group Jethro Tull, at home, in the recording studio and on tour in America. First broadcast 11 January 1980

programme.title: Sight & Sound in Concert: Supertramp
programme.desc: Bob Harris introduces a 1977 performance by the rock band, as Rodger Hodgson and Rick Davis perform hits including Dreamer, Give a Little Bit and Bloody Well Right

programme.title: TOTP2: Genesis
programme.desc: A celebration of the rock group that delves into the archives for performances by the band and its members past and present, including clips of solo material by Peter Gabriel and Phil Collins as well as Mike Rutherford's side project Mike & the Mechanics. Featuring I Can't Dance, I Know What I Like, No Self Control, In the Air Tonight, Many Too Many and The Living Years

programme.title: Losgannan/Froglets
programme.desc: Ziff, Pipp and Button become painters using honey, colourful petals and an old pizza box!

programme.title: Na Clangairean
programme.desc: When Beag Chlangair is out fishing on the music-boat, he catches something unusual

programme.title: Na Clangairean
programme.desc: The Clangers argue about who's faster and, so as to find out who indeed is faster, they race

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean (Puffin Rock)
programme.desc: Oona spends the day following the seagulls, learning all sorts of facts about their behaviour. The information comes in very useful when Baba gets himself in a spot of bother

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean (Puffin Rock)
programme.desc: Oona and Sily undertake the very important task of finding Bernie a new but suitable shell

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Flop and Bing have been playing hide and seek, and Bing has been been having so much fun that he does not want the game to end

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing is building a sandcastle and is joined by Pando, who has brought his toy dumper truck to help with the construction

programme.title: Peicein/Petit
programme.desc: Petit's mother scolds him for telling a nurse that she has a big nose. From that moment on, Petit starts telling white lies that grow little by little until they turn into big lies - and cause a huge misunderstanding

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pol Ploc (The Rubbish World of Dave Spud)
programme.desc: The Spuds launch a rescue mission when Fuzzypeg eats the flea that Dave is pet-sitting for Lil Sue. Will they reach Mr Grumps before he is dissolved like food inside Fuzzypeg’s tummy?

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pol Ploc (The Rubbish World of Dave Spud)
programme.desc: Dave has a bad day and tells a news reporter that Grimsby is rubbish. When his words are aired on the news, his whole seaside town turns against him. With the help of his friends and a rogue fox, Dave makes amends by shooting a film about all the great things that Grimsby has to offer.

programme.title: Na Saboteurs
programme.desc: Ten-year-old Magaidh is unhappy that her father has invited local teacher Ms Dunlop round for dinner. Magaidh's friend Dean is trying to pluck up the courage to ask her to the local dance. After he heads round to her house, Magaidh doesn't give him a chance to finish his question before Ms Dunlop arrives for the evening. Magaidh hatches a plot to sabotage the imminent date and enlists Dean's help. Burnt dinners, spoilt wine, soaking wet clothes and a very happy dog lead to an eventful evening for the two youngsters

programme.title: An Sgoil
programme.desc: A school inspector pays a surprise visit to Sgoil Taobh na Cloiche. It’s not looking good for Mr MacFhraing, although Mr Strachan seems to have found his feet. What could possibly go wrong?

programme.title: A’ Chùil
programme.desc: Choirstaidh presents from her studio A' Chùil to show a great way of changing up clothes

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC Alba

programme.title: Seachd Là (News Review)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: Iain MacKinnon presents a mix of hymns, psalms and readings from Coats in Paisley. Among the singers performing are Rachel Walker, Iain 'Costello' Maciver, Rona MacLeod & Duncan MacLeod, and the choir Soisgeul, conducted by Gareth Fuller

programme.title: Eòrpa
programme.desc: Reports on some of the most important issues from Scotland and across Europe

programme.title: Gàrradh Phàdruig
programme.desc: Peter MacQueen still has a lot of planting to get done in Glasgow, and gets to work on a hanging basket full of vibrant flowers and shows off his new garden ornament

programme.title: Miann na Maighdinn-mara: Eilean a' Cheo/Mermaid Tales: Isle of Skye
programme.desc: Kate MacLeod continues her journey around the south end of the Isle of Skye and the scenery just keeps getting better and better. She sails through Kylerhea in the company of a whale, makes her way underground to a special cave, and swims in the shadow of the famous Cuillins. Kate also visits another castle which is said to have strong connections to the warrior queen, Sgathach, and she gets an opportunity to swim in Loch nan Dùbhraichean

programme.title: Rudan Beaga Bòidheach le Niamh Mackinnon
programme.desc: Niamh and Kait try out mindfulness in Skye. In Gaelic

programme.title: Cuirm Aig Celtic 2026 (Òran Ùr)
programme.desc: A special concert at Celtic Connections celebrating 25 years of new Gaelic songs, presented by Joy Dunlop. Building on BBC Radio nan Gàidheal's Òran Ùr project that showcased Gaelic songs written since the turn of the century, the concert is curated by Gillebride MacMillan and features performances from stars of Gaelic singing and contemporary songwriting as well as various surprise guests

programme.title: Na Sgiobairean mu Dheireadh (The Last Captains)
programme.desc: With lobster season looming, captains fight fatigue and the elements to fill crab and scallop quotas before time - and luck - run out

programme.title: Samhradh air Sàl
programme.desc: Summer fishing trips off the Isle of Lewis with Angus Mackay and friends. In Gaelic

programme.desc: Having seen the frogs by the pond, Oona asks May to teach her how to jump really high

programme.desc: Oona and Silky struggle to agree on a game that they can both play and enjoy equally well

programme.title: Boban is Fuigheag
programme.desc: When Boban and Fuigheag go camping in the back garden, something from outside creates a shadow on the tent. Fuigheag is worried about what it might be

programme.title: Losgannan/Froglets
programme.desc: The Froglets transform kitchen cloths into mats for a day of fun yoga and gymnastics.

programme.title: Meaban is Moo
programme.desc: Linda asks Meaban and Moo to find three rectangular items. Their hut is full of junk so the pair need to have a good rummage in order to find them

programme.title: Dòtaman
programme.desc: Recorded in 1989, this edition of Dotaman explores the topic of noses in the fun, creative and colourful company of Donnie and Rhoda

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Abigail MacIver reads Blas na Dachaigh by Maryam Hassan, illustrated by Anna Wilson and translated into Gaelic by Donald John MacLeod

programme.title: An Teaghlach Rìoghail an Ath-dhoras (The Royals Next Door)
programme.desc: The school fair is going to take place and Stella and the children have to bake for it. The Queen wants to use a delicious royal recipe, but how will they get on?

programme.title: An Teaghlach Rìoghail an Ath-dhoras (The Royals Next Door)
programme.desc: The King and the Mayor’s competiveness puts the whole town in danger, but can this rivalry also save the town?

programme.title: An Sgoil
programme.desc: After last year's school inspection, the school board has appointed a new deputy head, Ms Dallas, to keep an eye on Mr Mackle

programme.title: Bogaisean is Gumbalan (Bottersnikes and Gumbles)
programme.desc: When Troig is mean about the twins’ singing, they decide to go in search of folk who’ll appreciate their talents

programme.title: Bogaisean is Gumbalan (Bottersnikes and Gumbles)
programme.desc: Bonns’s day hasn’t gone too well as she has stretched so far that she has “spanged” herself. At the end of the day, however, she learns that she can be helpful to her friends

programme.title: Saor no Daor
programme.desc: Christy and Zach compete to curate the perfect day out for each other in towns and cities around Scotland, one with a budget of £150, the other with just £40. In Gaelic

programme.title: Brunch le Abigail
programme.desc: Abigail MacIver takes the audience through her favourite brunch recipes

programme.title: SpeakGaelic

programme.title: Rathad Ramsay

programme.title: Eòrpa
programme.desc: Angela Maclean’s award-winning 2016 report, featuring interviews with former detainees at the notorious US prison at Guantanamo Bay

programme.title: Trusadh - Clann a' Mhansa An La An-De (Children of the Manse)
programme.desc: Broadcaster Cathy Macdonald talks to sons and daughters of the manse, who were brought up during a time when the church had far-reaching influence. She speaks to the Rev Iver Martin, who followed in his father's footsteps and became a minister, and who is now the Moderator of the Free Church of Scotland

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: Julie Fowlis and Muireann NicAmhlaoibh visit the Isle of Lewis to discover more about traditional music. Accompanying them from Ireland are renowned traditional singer Lillis O'Laoire and flautist Louise Mulcahy

programme.title: Cuirm@Celtic: Capercaillie & the BBC SSO
programme.desc: A landmark collaboration fusing innovative Skye band Niteworks with the BBC Scottish Symphony Orchestra. Recorded at Glasgow Royal Concert Hall as part of Celtic Connections

programme.title: Glan Fhèin
programme.desc: Betty proves how adept she is at blowing her mating call. She is wooed by a celebrity admirer - an outdoorsman who would travel to the ends of the Earth for her. Daniel makes a major declaration of love, Lewis Fraser does his best to break up the business and tensions simmer as Angela and Katie clean an outdoor sauna together. The clock is ticking, and it will take something special to stop the company being snatched away. In Gaelic with English subtitles

programme.desc: Ellen MacDonald reads Harriet an Nighean as Làidire san t-Saoghal by Ben Lerwill, illustrated by Beatrice Blue and translated into Gaelic by Donald John MacLeod

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pol Ploc (The Rubbish World of Dave Spud)
programme.desc: Dave discovers that his body is a musical instrument and is asked to replace a band member in his favourite group, the Whippets

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pol Ploc (The Rubbish World of Dave Spud)
programme.desc: Grimsby is putting on its annual community play and Anna wants the role of princess. Cast as a witch instead, the Spuds set out to prove that witches can have happy endings, too

programme.title: Strì
programme.desc: The CBBC Alba presenters embark on another challenge, but it remains to be seen who will emerge victorious as they are joined by Mhairi

programme.title: Mystique No 375
programme.desc: Short drama Mystique No 375, set in Largs, south-west Scotland. Ten-year-old Kriselle lives with her father, who manages a rundown seaside theatre. Kriselle is wise beyond her years, and has a hankering for being on stage herself - but the theatre is fast losing money and may have to close down. With Cailean Collier and Caramarie Maccalman

programme.title: A’ Chùil
programme.desc: Choirstaidh takes a look at the famous artist Hilma Af Klint, and shows you how to make your own picture in her style

programme.desc: Peter MacQueen can no longer ignore the mess of the front garden and he finally gets to work tidying it up. To make sure he stays on track, he enlists the help of plant enthusiast Mìcheal Bauer

programme.title: Miann na Maighdinn-mara: Eilean a' Cheo/Mermaid Tales: Isle of Skye
programme.desc: Kate Macleod visits Rubha an Dùnain in the company of Coinneach Maclean where she learns a little about Viking history. She also meets piper Decker Forrest, who tells her all about the famous MacCrimmon pipers. She then gets an opportunity to swim and cook with Clare Coghill, who is making a name for herself as an expert chef, and visits famous Skye landmarks Neist Point, Talisker Bay and the Ascrib Islands

programme.title: Bill agus an Spitfire
programme.desc: Bill Innes first saw a Spitfire flying overhead when he was seven years old in 1940. Filled with hero worship for the daring pilots of the Battle of Britain, he dreamed of roaming the clouds in such a thrilling machine. As a student, he was selected to join the RAF air reserve squadron and to his delight, when the time came for him to do National Service, was accepted for pilot training in the RAF. Bill went on to become a pilot, and although he spent his working life flying different types of aircraft, he never managed to take to the skies in the iconic Spitfire - something he now intends to correct

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Donald Macsween, aka Sweeny, is expecting more calves than ever to be born, but unforeseen problems lie ahead. With the cost of items like fertiliser rising constantly, he decides to revive an old tradition by collecting seaweed from the nearby beach to take back to spread on his croft. He visits a research farm outside Edinburgh to find out if seaweed can be fed to his cows, and also sees the chambers where methane levels from cows are measured. After some terrible setbacks, Sweeny goes to the lamb sales in Dingwall

programme.title: Clive Myrie's Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: Clive travels to the Dominican Republic's capital city, Santo Domingo, where he meets one of the country's top baseball scouts, who gives him much-needed advice on his swing. In the historic old town, he meets artisans who are reviving intricate, traditional craftwork. Clive visits the market with one of the country's top chefs to pick up the ingredients for a seven-meat Dominican dish

programme.title: Will Insects Thrive or Just Survive?
programme.desc: Insects play a crucial role in food production and the spread of disease, as well as being keystone species in many ecosystems. Zoologist Dr Tim Cockerill discusses how climate change affects them.

programme.title: The Media Show

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.desc: It's an important year for the city of Hull, in northern England. It's celebrating more than 800 years of maritime history with a raft of new museum openings that it's hoped will make it a world-class visitor attraction. Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to see the opening of the restored Spurn Lightship - literally a lighthouse on a ship - which was a mainstay on the waterways there for 50 years. She meets the people involved in the huge regeneration effort and finds out why the city's been the punchline of jokes for so many years.

programme.title: Inside Hungary's Political Earthquake
programme.desc: A weekly programme from the BBC World Service, bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world by BBC Eye, the World Service's critically acclaimed investigative unit, and featuring expert global correspondents getting to the heart of the biggest international stories. This week, in the aftermath of a political earthquake in Hungary, Stephanie Hegarty explores whether the downfall of Viktor Orbán could be a warning sign for populist leaders everywhere, and BBC Eye investigates the Russian nationalists who are taking the law into their own hands.

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: The journalist in conversation with people who influence our political thinking about what has shaped theirs

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: House of Commons proceedings in Westminster Hall including debates on regional accents and social mobility, the impact of the Peak Cluster Pipeline, the impact of waste management sites on local communities, the potential merits of a VAT reduction pilot for hospitality businesses in Northern Ireland, and on estate regeneration, from Wednesday 15 July.

programme.title: Ten Minute Rule Bill on the Registration of Stillbirths
programme.desc: Coverage of the introduction to the House of Commons of a ten minute rule bill, tabled by Labour MP Jim McMahon, on the registration of stillbirths in England and Wales, on Wednesday 15 July.

programme.title: Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps Debate
programme.desc: Coverage of the debate in the House of Commons on a motion to proscribe the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps as a threat to national security, from Wednesday 15 July.

programme.title: House of Lords - Predatory Market Tactics Question
programme.desc: Coverage of a private notice question in the House of Lords on protections for shareholders from unauthorised overseas firms and mini-tender offers, tabled by the non-affiliated peer Baroness Altmann, on Wednesday 15 July.

programme.title: Sir David Amess Summer Adjournment Debate
programme.desc: Coverage of the debate in the House of Commons marking the start of the summer recess and named in honour of Sir David Amess, the Southend West MP who was murdered in 2021, from Thursday 16 July.

programme.title: Senedd Cymru - Welsh Parliament - Legislation Statement
programme.desc: Coverage of the statement in the Senedd, the Welsh Parliament, by the first minister, Rhun ap Iorwerth, on the Welsh government's plans for legislation, from Tuesday 14 July.

programme.title: Gibraltar Statement
programme.desc: Coverage of the statement in the House of Commons by the foreign office minister, Stephen Doughty, on the UK-EU Treaty in respect of Gibraltar, on Thursday 16 July.

programme.title: House of Lords - Civil Service Pensions
programme.desc: Coverage of the statement in the House of Lords by cabinet office minister Baroness Anderson of Stoke-on-Trent on civil service pensions, on Monday 13 July.

programme.title: Prime Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the House of Commons to Keir Starmer, the prime minister, on Wednesday 15 July.

programme.title: Welsh First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Questions in the Senedd, the Welsh Parliament, to the first minister Rhun ap Iorwerth, on Tuesday 14 July.

programme.title: Foreign Affairs Committee with Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper
programme.desc: Coverage of the Foreign Affairs select committee session with evidence from the foreign secretary, Yvette Cooper, and the interim permanent secretary at the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office, Nick Dyer, from Tuesday 14 July.

programme.title: Health Committee on Maternity Care
programme.desc: Coverage of the Health and Social Care select committee session on the reviews of maternity care in the NHS in England with evidence from Baroness Amos, chair of the National Maternity and Neonatal Investigation and Donna Ockenden, chair of the Independent Review into Maternity Services at the Nottingham University Hospital NHS Trust, on Wednesday 15 July.

programme.title: Housing, Communities and Local Government Committee with Steve Reed
programme.desc: Coverage of the Housing, Communities and Local Government select committee session on the work of the ministry with evidence from the housing, communities and local government secretary, Steve Reed, and the permanent secretary, Will Garton, from Tuesday 14 July.

programme.title: Ten Minute Rule Bill on Freedom of Information
programme.desc: Coverage of the introduction to the House of Commons of a Bill under the ten minute rule, tabled by Green MP Siân Berry, on applying freedom of information legislation to the royal family, the royal archives and the duchies of Cornwall and Lancaster, from Tuesday 14 July.

programme.title: Statement on the Death of Ann Widdecombe
programme.desc: Coverage of the statement in the House of Commons by home secretary Shabana Mahmood on the death of Ann Widdecombe and the security of MPs and politicians, on Monday 13 July

programme.title: Statement on the State of Nature and Climate 2026
programme.desc: Coverage of the statement in the House of Commons by nature minister Mary Creagh, on the state of nature and climate 2026, on Monday 13 July

programme.title: Switzerland Free Trade Statement
programme.desc: Coverage of the statement in the House of Commons by the business and trade minister Chris Bryant on the enhanced free trade agreement with Switzerland, on Tuesday 14 July.

programme.title: Live Local Government Statement
programme.desc: Coverage of the statement in the House of Commons by the local government secretary, Steve Reed, on local government reorganisation, on Thursday 16 July.

programme.title: Prime Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the House of Commons to Keir Starmer, the prime minister, on Wednesday 15 July.

programme.title: Welsh First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Questions in the Senedd, the Welsh Parliament, to the first minister Rhun ap Iorwerth, on Tuesday 14 July.

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: House of Commons proceedings in Westminster Hall including debates on the impact of changes to the National Planning Policy Framework and on capital funding for Changing Places toilets, from Thursday 16 July.
