programme.title: Reporting Scotland: News at Seven
programme.desc: Reports from BBC newsrooms across the country with analysis and discussion on the big stories of the day

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea and Will Kirk travel around the UK fixing historical treasures, with this edition seeing them visit the Scottish Boat Building School in Irvine, North Ayrshire, to restore a three-piece canoe designed in the 1930s. Will also calls in to Barr Castle in Galston, East Ayrshire, to repair a windowpane with a hand inscription by Robert Burns

programme.title: Island Crossings
programme.desc: CalMac engineer Fergus Barlow races in the annual Mull car rally, and his brother Craig has travelled all the way from New Zealand to be his navigator. Meanhile, the ferry operator's CEO Robbie Drummond joins the engineering team of the MV Caledonian Isles to find out what challenges they face, and looks on as things start to go wrong in the engine room

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Crews are mobilised to a large building fire in Glasgow city centre, with concerns that people are trapped inside. As fire and rescue battle the blaze, Stewart of the Paramedic Response Unit endures heavy rain and prepares to treat casualties. Meanwhile, Ayr-based crew Sophie and Shelby treat a man who has been drinking alcohol and has sustained a concerning head injury, but is adamant he doesn't want to go to hospital.

programme.title: Still Game
programme.desc: Jack and Victor visit the local park, which has gone dangerously downhill - but Winston has a plan to move it up in the world, Comedy, written by and starring Ford Kiernan and Greg Hemphill

programme.title: Scot Squad
programme.desc: Volunteer Officer Ken Beattie wrestles with a dodgy dealer and the dilemma of whether he is or isn't offering him a bribe. PCs Hugh McKirdy and Surjit Singh pick up a furry friend on their travels, and PC Charlie McIntosh introduces PC Jane MacKay to a festival called the Butterfist

programme.title: Greetings from Scotland
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary taking a humorous look at the early days of the nation's tourist industry, with the Scottish tourist board becoming a government-funded body in 1969, with a remit was to promote Scotland as a holiday destination

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: It's shearing time for the Imries, but their minds are on their next big event - a family music festival. They need this event to make a profit and are relying on last-minute ticket sales on the day. In Dumfries and Galloway, after the shock of losing 300 acres of prime grazing, Nick's neighbour offers to lease him some land, giving him an idea for a new business venture. On Papa Westray it's shearing time and another first for Paul. He's been on a shearing course and is tackling the job himself. The whole family takes part in Papay Fun Weekend, a highlight of the island community's calendar

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Ruth Vichos is in her vertical garden where she has strawberries, blossoms and various climbing varieties all demonstrating what you can be produced with stacking systems and plants that will follow vertical surfaces. Brian Cunningham reviews the wild flower beds he sowed in April and which will become a great resource for pollinators and the rest of the garden's ecosystem

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Stevie and Pete of the Special Operations Response Team join the other emergency services to help a man suffering a mental-health crisis who is threatening to harm himself in a public place. Dunoon-based paramedic Ben responds to a potentially life-threatening emergency in the shape of a man who has blood a clot in his lungs

programme.title: Reporting Scotland: News at Seven
programme.desc: Reports from BBC newsrooms across the country with analysis and discussion on the big stories of the day

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond prepare meals using traditional Scottish produce, including a twice-baked Mull cheese soufflé with Ayrshire bacon and kale

programme.title: The Mountain
programme.desc: The team struggles to keep the resort open when snowstorms hit in the new year. Burns Night means a busy time for Spud the piper and Fraser the butcher, while the mountain men come off the hill to take part in a 24-hour endurance race. Also, ski-hire-shop owners Brian and Carol Muirhead reveal the importance of being up early to catch the crowds before they head to the slopes

programme.title: The Mountain
programme.desc: After the new year snowstorms, the villagers grab their shovels and help get the mountain ready for the skiers, and after 10 months off with two broken ankles, barman Brett can't wait to get back on his snowboard. Londoners Simon and Tanja Hughes, who moved to Aviemore to manage a set of holiday chalets, reveal how shortly after arriving they also took over the Super Panther pub. Now they have decided, in the depths of winter, to throw a beach party

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The designers pair up to create uplifting spaces for a homelessness charity in London, tackling an arts and crafts room, a group workshop room, a wellness area and an employment suite. The charity's ambassador, Dara Ó Briain, joins Alan to check in on the designers, before Michelle Ogundehin and guest judge Sophie Robinson critique the spaces

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Tyler visits Harry in prison, leading him to consider who could have framed his dad, while Caitlin revels in the online backlash that Scarlett is receiving. A terrified Lydia receives her diagnosis at the hospital, and a stunned Jodie is left reeling from her scan

programme.title: Two Doors Down
programme.desc: Over at Beth and Eric's, the neighbours are getting stuck into a family sized box of doughnuts when the new health-conscious residents, Ash and Iqbal, turn up to introduce themselves. Comedy, starring Arabella Weir and Alex Norton

programme.title: Greetings from Scotland
programme.desc: Part two of two. Two events that transformed Scottish tourism in the autumn of 1995 - the release of Braveheart and the first-ever easyJet flight from Luton to Glasgow

programme.title: Reporting Scotland: News at Seven
programme.desc: Reports from BBC newsrooms across the country with analysis and discussion on the big stories of the day

programme.title: My Kind of Town
programme.desc: Ian Hamilton heads to the once-popular South Ayrshire seaside resort of Girvan, of which he has happy childhood memories and wants to find out how much it has changed. From the more traditional fun of the boating lake and the games arcade, to newer aspects of the town like accessible bicycles and bubble football, there is something for everyone

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On Papa Westray, Paul's visiting nephews are roped into farm work, while Paul and Mel have lots to do for their long-awaited wedding day. It's a concerning time for Sally as some of her ewes are struck down by a devastating mystery illness. In Fife, Andy and Jo increase their loan to install another holiday pod and take 400 lambs to market to raise some much-needed cash

programme.title: The Man in the Mask: An Orkney Murder
programme.desc: In 1994, Bangladeshi waiter Shamsuddin Mahmood was shot dead at an Indian restaurant in Kirkwall in the Orkneys. It was the first murder there for a generation, and 15-year-old schoolboy Michael Ross became the police's prime suspect. Journalist Hazel Martin begins her investigation into a case full of twists and turns, getting hold of secret recordings of Ross in prison

programme.title: The Man in the Mask: An Orkney Murder
programme.desc: Hazel Martin investigates the character of Michael Ross, the man convicted of his Shamsuddin Mahmood's murder, to assess if a 15-year-old would have been capable of such a brutal and cold-blooded act. Hazel also explores the background of the victim and uncovers a story of two hitmen who used to live near him before he came to Orkney. Could they have committed the murder instead?

programme.title: The Man in the Mask: An Orkney Murder
programme.desc: Twelve years after the murder of Shamsuddin Mahmood, a new eyewitness came forward saying he saw Michael Ross with a balaclava and a gun just before the murder just before the murder. Hazel Martin investigates whether this story holds up, then tests out Michael Ross's alibi, only to hear a shocking revelation from an investigator on the case

programme.title: Ray Bradshaw's Bald Ginger
programme.desc: A stand-up show from the Stand Comedy Club in Glasgow, with the comedian telling stories of the bizarre situations he got into while trying to find people who look like him

programme.title: Reporting Scotland: News at Seven
programme.desc: Reports from BBC newsrooms across the country with analysis and discussion on the big stories of the day

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year 2024
programme.desc: Judges Anna Campbell-Jones, Banjo Beale and Danny Campbell visit contenders from the Highlands and Islands. The contenders are a fishing lodge near Aviemore which dates back to the 18th century, a modern self build near Stornoway designed to be symmetrical and considerate to the environment, and a converted 1900s croft in Lewis filled with second hand finds and upcycled furniture

programme.title: Island Crossings
programme.desc: CalMac engineer Fergus Barlow races in the annual Mull car rally, and his brother Craig has travelled all the way from New Zealand to be his navigator. Meanhile, the ferry operator's CEO Robbie Drummond joins the engineering team of the MV Caledonian Isles to find out what challenges they face, and looks on as things start to go wrong in the engine room

programme.title: Caroline Walker: Women's Work
programme.desc: Documentary following the Internationally renowned Scottish artist over four years as she makes large-scale paintings representing the unseen, often undervalued aspects of women's labour, from hospitals to hotels, nail bars to nursery settings. As the film follows Walker through pregnancy and the birth of her second child, it provides an exploration of the role of the artist mother, until recently viewed as taboo in contemporary art creativity.

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: While Kelly-Marie helps Michaela remember her dad, she is left reeling when Chris confronts her over a decision made without him. Mikey suspects Mack is avoiding him, Mulvaney is concerned at the prospect of Harry's arrival, and Lenny takes a different approach with Lydia

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Winnie is ready to hit the road - but first needs to pass her driving test. Naturally, Agnes and the gang volunteer to help her practise, and chaos soon follows. With Winnie behind the wheel and Buster instructing, Finglas becomes a danger zone. Comedy, co-written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll, with Danny O'Carroll and guest stars Shobu Kapoor and Marek Larwood

programme.title: David Wilson's Crime Files
programme.desc: The criminologist investigates the origins of Glasgow's alternative motto of No Mean City, looking at gang culture past and present. Investigative journalist Fiona Walker presents a short film about the 1930s era of gang culture - Glasgow's Peaky Blinders

programme.title: Reporting Scotland: News at Seven
programme.desc: Reports from BBC newsrooms across the country with analysis and discussion on the big stories of the day

programme.title: Hidden Lives
programme.desc: Peter Ross experiences the pomp and pageantry of the Bo'ness Fair, said to be the largest children's fair in Europe. Each year since 1897 a local schoolgirl has been crowned Fair Queen in a ceremony which, arguably, means more to the town than a real coronation would to the nation. It features hand-built plywood castles bigger than houses, custom-made dresses that would be the envy of any young royal, and a grand procession with bands and floats that is a sort of Scottish Mardi Gras

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Brian Cunningham and Kirsty Wilson work on the garden's borders, replanting, filling gaps and replacing losses all around the plot. Kirsty will also be planting in the border behind Beechgrove's main pond, where other gaps have appeared, and she is building a new structure to create a habitat for pollinators

programme.title: Scotland's Greatest Escape
programme.desc: Father and daughter Graham and Aimee from Dumfriesshire try out three well-being retreats, visiting the elegant Stobo spa, having a close encounter at Sheepy Sleepovers and a staying in a handcrafted Romani caravan at Roulotte Retreat. JJ Chalmers and Grado present

programme.title: Granite Harbour
programme.desc: A missing persons case turns into a double murder investigation and age-old tensions between the traveller and traditional Aberdonian communities threaten to explode. Davis and the team call on the help of an experienced liaison officer to find a way into the travelling community as understanding it quickly is pivotal to finding the truth. Return of the crime drama, starring Romario Simpson and Hannah Donaldson

programme.title: Scot Squad
programme.desc: Volunteer Officer Ken Beattie wrestles with a dodgy dealer and the dilemma of whether he is or isn't offering him a bribe. PCs Hugh McKirdy and Surjit Singh pick up a furry friend on their travels, and PC Charlie McIntosh introduces PC Jane MacKay to a festival called the Butterfist

programme.title: Cumming to the Cabaret
programme.desc: Alan Cumming provides a behind-the-scenes glimpse into the eclectic group of artists at his Cabaret-themed bar and club in New York City's East Village as they navigate their personal and professional lives in today's turbulent political landscape

programme.title: Cumming to the Cabaret
programme.desc: Alan heads to Hollywood to receive his star on the Walk of Fame, while Daphne enlists Michelle and drag king Cunning to join her in a new show at the club

programme.title: Des Doesn't Do - Horror
programme.desc: Comedian Des Clarke is not a fan of the horror genre, so fellow comedian Robert Florence tries to overturn his dislike by deconstructing the genre. Along the way, the presenter faces a film fright night, goes behind the scenes of Scotland's scary movie industry and joins a ghost tour around Glamis Castle, Angus

programme.title: Loop
programme.desc: In late 2022 Leyla Josephine's debut poetry book, In Public/In Private was published, with a whirlwind tour around England, Scotland and Northern Ireland. This documentary witnesses the performance poet at the top of her game, as she pauses for breath in her adopted home town of Prestwick. In an exclusive recording from the beach, Leyla reads Questions I Have for Birds - and gets ready for her much-anticipated, packed homecoming gig at Glasgow's King Tut's Wah Wah Hut

programme.title: Reporting Scotland: News at Seven
programme.desc: Reports from BBC newsrooms across the country with analysis and discussion on the big stories of the day

programme.title: Scotland's Greatest Escape
programme.desc: Father and daughter Graham and Aimee from Dumfriesshire try out three well-being retreats, visiting the elegant Stobo spa, having a close encounter at Sheepy Sleepovers and a staying in a handcrafted Romani caravan at Roulotte Retreat. JJ Chalmers and Grado present

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Crews are mobilised to a large building fire in Glasgow city centre, with concerns that people are trapped inside. As fire and rescue battle the blaze, Stewart of the Paramedic Response Unit endures heavy rain and prepares to treat casualties. Meanwhile, Ayr-based crew Sophie and Shelby treat a man who has been drinking alcohol and has sustained a concerning head injury, but is adamant he doesn't want to go to hospital.

programme.title: Ooh the Banter
programme.desc: Jack Docherty delves into the BBC archives for a celebration of Scottish comedy, joined by Elaine C Smith, Alex Norton, Karen Dunbar, Lawrence Chaney, Brian Cox, Alan Cumming and Forbes Masson. Jack draws a line from the early days of Scottish comedy on TV from the McFlannels, Stanley Baxter and Lex McLean through to the modern generation of quickfire online sensations, and finds that colourful language, authenticity, irreverence and flair for creative insults remain the cornerstones of Scottish humour

programme.title: Still Game
programme.desc: Jack and Victor worry that Bert has been suffering since his wife died, while Winston has acquired a limp and a home help. Comedy, starring Ford Kiernan and Greg Hemphill

programme.title: Two Doors Down
programme.desc: Over at Beth and Eric's, the neighbours are getting stuck into a family sized box of doughnuts when the new health-conscious residents, Ash and Iqbal, turn up to introduce themselves. Comedy, starring Arabella Weir and Alex Norton

programme.title: Rip It Up
programme.desc: Examining Scotland's unique pop music history, beginning with performers revealing how they went from bedroom dreamers to international superstars. Featuring contributions from solo artists like Lulu and Donovan, as well as members of pioneering groups including the Skids, Nazareth, the Incredible String Band, Josef K, the Average White Band, Middle of the Road, the Rezillos, the Beatstalkers and the Sensational Alex Harvey Band.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: It's tense in the Beale household when Ian's lawyer advises that he pleads guilty at his upcoming plea hearing. Peter and Lauren encourage Ian to listen to advice, but Kathy later insists he should plead innocent. Max makes an offer, and the Fox-Truemans prepare to welcome Denise home

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise struggles to settle back into family life, it's the day of Ian's plea hearing, and Mark raises suspicions

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise insists to Chelsea that she wants to meet Sheila today. Vicki and Ross return from honeymoon to Sharon's delight, but things soon turn awkward when Zack arrives, and Mark's doubts start to grow

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mark puts Lauren to the test, Cleo tells Vicki, Ross and Sharon that she doesn't want Joel to live with her, and Gina attempts to right a wrong

programme.title: The Notebook
programme.desc: An elderly man reads a love story to a woman in a nursing home, from an old notebook full of sentimental scribblings about passionate young couple Allie and Noah, whose fledgling affair in the 1940s is smothered almost before it has begun when Allie's parents do not approve of her partner as he does not fit in with their other wealthy friends. Romantic drama, starring Ryan Gosling, Rachel McAdams, Gena Rowlands and James Garner

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: Drama, starring Aimee Lou Wood and Nabhaan Rizwan as best friends retreating from reality into a world of movies. However, change is coming to their lives whether they like it or not

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: While Noa attends a taster day for his new job in Bristol, Evie pulls out all the stops for Film Club - there's a lot riding on tonight. Drama, starring Aimee Lou Wood and Nabhaan Rizwan

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: Evie is still reeling from the message she saw on Noa's phone. But when the two are left alone together, something extraordinary happens. Is it the start of a new journey for Evie? Drama, starring Aimee Lou Wood and Nabhaan Rizwan

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: Evie throws herself headlong at life, but Suz and Josh worry it's too much, too soon. Noa realises he has to face up to his feelings. Drama, starring Aimee Lou Wood, Suranne Jones, Adam Long and Nabhaan Rizwan

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: Evie retreats to the safety of the garage, but with a new understanding, Suz and Izzie create a unique version of Film Club to bring back Evie's smile

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: The day has finally come for Noa to leave, but Evie might not let him go that easily and it is not the only big decision she has to face

programme.title: Things You Should Have Done
programme.desc: A overly dependent young woman's parents die in a car accident, forcing her to fend for herself. It transpires she will inherit the house on condition she completes a list of 'Things You Should Have Done' left by her parents. Chi's highly strung Auntie Karen wants nothing to do with her 'pest of a niece', but very much wants the house she feels is rightfully hers. Offbeat, dysfunctional family sitcom, written by and starring Lucia Keskin, with Selin Hizli

programme.title: Things You Should Have Done
programme.desc: Chi spectacularly fails a trial period at the pharmacy with Karen, but Dave kindly lets her shadow him on his delivery rounds. On a drop-off to a retirement home Chi finds her tribe - old people. She also falls in love with Linda, an elderly lady who seems to think Chi is her husband. Dave has a health scare and Karen starts to think she might be the fertility issue. Offbeat, dysfunctional family sitcom, starring Lucia Keskin, Selin Hizli and Daniel Fearn

programme.title: Things You Should Have Done
programme.desc: Chi is determined the family will celebrate Pancake Day, and inspires Gary to try and reunite with his wife. Dave confides in Lucas about his health scare and they vow to make the most of every moment. Family shenanigans and suspect pancake making result in a trip to A&E, where Karen enjoys the attention too much, Dave makes a nuisance of himself and Chi has a health scare of her own

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards 2024
programme.desc: Darren Harriott presents as 30 hopefuls compete across six heats to earn the title of best British comic. The competition begins in Leicester where comedian, playwright and actor Sukh Kaur Ojla, writer Jack Rooke and comic Babatunde Aleshe judge stand-up performances from Dane Buckley, Kathy Rivett, Hasan Al-Habib, Sharon Wanjohi and Holly Ludlow

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Spider-Man: Homecoming
programme.desc: Peter Parker balances life as an ordinary high school student with his superhero alter-ego Spider-Man, while waiting in vain to join his mentor Iron Man on a world-saving mission. Determined to prove he is ready for bigger challenges, he investigates a spree of crimes committed with advanced weapons - leading him into a battle with a ruthless winged enemy on the hunt for priceless technology. Superhero adventure, starring Tom Holland, Michael Keaton and Robert Downey Jr

programme.title: Spider-Man: Far from Home
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the Avengers' climactic battle for the fate of the Universe, teenage webslinger Peter Parker just wants life to get back to normal, all while dodging his day-to-day responsibilities. A school trip across Europe seems just the thing - but he ends up recruited by spy agency SHIELD to battle an invasion by elemental monsters from another dimension alongside new, unknown hero Mysterio. However, the teen from Queens begins to doubt himself. Superhero adventure sequel, starring Tom Holland, Jake Gyllenhaal, Zendaya and Samuel L Jackson

programme.title: Top Gear USA Special
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May travel to America for an interstate road trip, completing various challenges along the way. Starting in Miami, they each buy a car for less than $1,000 and drive to New Orleans, where they set out to give their vehicles away to people who suffered in Hurricane Katrina

programme.title: Eddie Hall: The Beast v the Mountain
programme.desc: Behind-the-scenes access to Hall's preparations for a Dubai bout with fellow former World's Strongest Man Hafthor Bjornsson, who gained fame as The Mountain in Game of Thrones. The pair were once close friends, but had a bitter falling out in 2017 and opted to settle their differences in the ring in what was dubbed as the heaviest boxing match in history. The documentary also looks at Eddie's personal story, which has seen him struggle with mental health issues earlier in his life as he obsessively chased his goals

programme.title: Small Town, Big Riot
programme.desc: Journalist Mobeen Azhar visits Kirkby, Merseyside to find out how a peaceful protest outside a hotel housing asylum seekers turned into a riot in February 2023 and uncovers the blueprint for a national wave of violent riots that took place across the country in the summer of 2024. Mobeen is soon confronted with serious allegations about what a group of asylum seekers have supposedly been up to in the small town, and attempts to uncover the truth but locals close ranks and warn each other against speaking to him, claiming Mobeen and the film crew will twist their words to make them look bad

programme.title: Small Town, Big Riot
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar's investigation reveals a white supremacist that was campaigning outside the Suites hotel ahead of the protest may have influenced the riot. He also heads further afield to Scampton, Lincolnshire where residents have been campaigning against the Home Office's planned asylum centre in the former RAF base

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. The rogue Zygon faction demands that all of the shape-shifting aliens disguised as humans must reveal their identities and pick a side in the coming war. While the Doctor and Osgood search for Clara, their enemies are searching for the mysterious Osgood Box, the contents of which could decide the fate of the Earth

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
programme.desc: With the web-slinger's identity now revealed to the world, Peter Parker asks Doctor Strange for magical help to undo the damage. However, a spell gone wrong unleashes dangerous foes from parallel universes on the world. Comic-book adventure sequel, starring Tom Holland, Zendaya and Benedict Cumberbatch, with Willem Dafoe, Jamie Foxx and Alfred Molina among the stars of past Spider-Man movies reprising their roles

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The three motoring enthusiasts head to Spain on a mission to help its struggling economy - but the convertible supercars they take are very expensive and anything but discreet. With Jeremy Clarkson in a McLaren MP4-12C, Richard Hammond driving a Ferrari 458 and James May behind the wheel of an Audi R8 V10, the trio encounter mountain passes, empty towns and an abandoned airport before creating their own street circuit in a deserted Madrid development. Plus, actor Benedict Cumberbatch sets a lap time in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Motor racing driver Lewis Hamilton demonstrates his abilities on the test track, and Richard Hammond tries his hand in a Formula One car. Jeremy Clarkson and James May assess historic vehicles to establish which one pioneered the control layout used in modern cars, and singer-songwriter James Blunt is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Rush
programme.desc: The 1976 Formula 1 Championship season is dominated by a fierce rivalry between two contrasting drivers, as daredevil English driver James Hunt and methodical Austrian defending champion Niki Lauda put their lives on the line to outdo each other on the track. Fact-based drama, starring Chris Hemsworth and Daniel Bruhl, with Olivia Wilde and Alexandra Maria Lara

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: After the attack on his best friends and threats against his sister, Mobeen is armed, dangerous and out for revenge. However, his attempts to make up for past mistakes see him pulled back into the world of unhinged crime boss Uncle Khan. Sitcom, co-written by and starring Guz Khan, with Art Malik

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: While trying to do a good deed by helping out at the local food bank, Mobeen is also under strict orders from Uncle Khan to launder money - and juggling the two things could leave him with a deadly debt that's impossible to pay

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: A drug deal goes wrong at the church where Mobeen is attending the funeral of one of his former teachers, leaving him with bigger things to worry about than trying to prevent Aks being excluded from school. Comedy, starring Guz Khan

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: With a contract out on Officer Harper's life, Mobeen steps in to try and save him and take him out of the firing line. Eight and Nate must stall the would-be assassin long enough for Mobeen and Harper to escape through the woods

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: The gang has to get out of Small Heath, but Mobeen needs to tie up loose ends and guarantee his sister's safety before the police or Khan's gang take him down. Comedy, starring Guz Khan, with Art Malik

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards 2024
programme.desc: Welsh comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean travels to Pontypridd in the heart of the south Wales Valleys to host the Wales and West heat. Louie McLean, Suri Ashish, Sarah Roberts, Paul Hilleard and Josh Harvey as Bernard Guff take to the stage at at Y Muni in a bid to impress judges Spencer Jones, Hannah Moulder and Babatunde Aleshe and book their place in the final.

programme.title: A Better Place
programme.desc: After his father's death, Archie inherits the failing family business - A Better Place Funeral Home. Archie, his sister Moira and cousin Ron try to stop the business falling into the hands of international corporate chain Sympathy Plus. Comedy short, starring Jacob Topen

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: The team heads north to Alaska, where days and nights of freezing weather must be endured to catch a glimpse of a wolverine. Some cheeky Arctic foxes are also spotted, and Steve Backshall resolves not to leave the state until he has seen a grizzly bear

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: The team continues the search for deadly animals in the Arizona desert, where Steve Backshall demonstrates the hunting prowess of the Harris hawk by acting as its prey. He also uses state-of-the-art technology to track down rattlesnakes, before heading to the mountains in search of a cougar

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: In an episode originally shown in 2011, Jeremy Clarkson celebrates the 50th anniversary of the Jaguar E-type by driving a modern-day interpretation of the classic sports car, and throws a typically low-key party involving live music, fighter planes and the Royal Marines. Richard Hammond is in South Africa, where he tests the Marauder, a bigger, more rugged alternative to the Humvee, and James May enlists the help of 2010 Winter Olympic skeleton gold medallist Amy Williams and former Intercontinental Rally Challenge champion Kris Meeke to try out a Mini rally car. Plus, the Stig takes the BMW 1 Series M Coupe around the test track and Alice Cooper gets behind the wheel of the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Part one of two. Tir Dhondy reveals the realities behind modern Las Vegas. The first episode explores the risk of crime faced by tourists and visits a pool party where massive screens show scores of live sports events from around the world so that partygoers can bet from the comfort of their sun lounger

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tir Dhondy goes further into the underbelly of Las Vegas to investigate how the reinvention of the city to pull in a new generation of visitors is affecting locals

programme.title: Ibiza: Secrets of the Party Island
programme.desc: Zara McDermott explores the island's economy, with access to luxury clubs, yachts and villas, as well as the local police, undercover detectives and emergency medics. In the first episode, she meets Wayne Lineker, Gary's little brother who has made it rich by providing clubbers with a day club pool party

programme.title: Ibiza: Secrets of the Party Island
programme.desc: Zara McDermott gets a closer look at how social media is changing crime. Along with a police team from St Josep, she attends a robbery of a high end villa at knife-point

programme.title: Ibiza: Secrets of the Party Island
programme.desc: Zara McDermott delves into the dark economy that keeps the drugs flowing in Ibiza, meeting with the drug supplier who never deals on the streets but feeds a seemingly never-ending supply of Class A drugs to demanding clientele all over the island. She also speaks to the police officers on the front line who are trying to cope with the excessive drug use, often allowing users with small supplies to walk away in order to target the dealers in the act of selling. Officers explain how social media has made their jobs much more difficult and Zara follows as they raid a luxury car rental company next to the main airport

programme.title: Ibiza: Secrets of the Party Island
programme.desc: Zara McDermott meets Spain's top lawyer when it comes to defending drug traffickers, Ricardo Alvarez-Ossorio, and joins the Spanish Civil Guard as they tackle organised crime and drug trafficking across the country. While on a night shift with an ambulance crew, Zara speaks to a nurse who explains how Ibiza is the ideal testing ground for new drugs

programme.title: Roman Kemp: The Fight for Young Lives
programme.desc: Broadcaster Roman Kemp knows first-hand the impact of serious mental health issues. In 2020, he made Our Silent Emergency in the wake of the death of a friend. He encouraged those in need to talk to friends, family and professionals, but now strives to move the conversation on from talking about mental health to discovering what action is needed to support those suffering

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards 2024
programme.desc: Award-winning comedian Laura Smyth hosts the southern heat from the Rivoli Ballrooms in south London. Lara Ricote, Holly Walsh and Babatunde Aleshe judge as Mushin Yesilada, Maia Tassalini, Limahi Germain, Alfie Packham and Frankie Thompson perform a set in hopes of going through to the grand final

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: In an episode originally shown in 2011, Jeremy Clarkson celebrates the 50th anniversary of the Jaguar E-type by driving a modern-day interpretation of the classic sports car, and throws a typically low-key party involving live music, fighter planes and the Royal Marines. Richard Hammond is in South Africa, where he tests the Marauder, a bigger, more rugged alternative to the Humvee, and James May enlists the help of 2010 Winter Olympic skeleton gold medallist Amy Williams and former Intercontinental Rally Challenge champion Kris Meeke to try out a Mini rally car. Plus, the Stig takes the BMW 1 Series M Coupe around the test track and Alice Cooper gets behind the wheel of the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall searches for deadly creatures in the swamps of Louisiana, finding alligators, cottonmouth snakes and predatory turtles

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall learns how to hypnotise sharks in the Bahamas and is given the opportunity to release some back into the wild

programme.title: Top Gear: Polar Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond compete in a land race from Resolute, Canada, to the North Pole in their most ambitious challenge to date. Pitting horse power against dog power they travel 450 miles across mountainous land masses and jagged sea ice at temperatures as low as minus 65C

programme.title: The Traitors Canada
programme.desc: The guests are shocked to find four players absent from the breakfast table, only to learn that their fate in the game is at the risk of being buried. While the remaining contestants attempt to save them from a grave fate, they quickly discover that not everyone will make it out alive

programme.title: The Traitors Canada
programme.desc: With suspicion intensifying inside the manor and a focus on one player in particular, another sees it as an opportunity to set their cunning plan into motion and make a swift strike for revenge

programme.title: The Traitors Canada
programme.desc: In the aftermath of an eventful Round Table and banishment, the remaining guests begin to examine the exchanges between certain players the night before. Another finds themself stranded and at the mercy of the Traitors

programme.title: The Traitors Canada
programme.desc: With a Faithful treading troubled waters with their fellow guests, the Traitors see an opportunity to set a trap disguised as an invitation to join their midst. The assassins wait with bated breath to see if their plan will succeed or if the player figures out their malicious intent

programme.title: My Old Ass
programme.desc: Teenager Elliott is poised to leave home and embark on the next chapter of her life. During a mushroom-fuelled trip, she comes face to face with her 39-year-old future self, who distills thought-provoking advice. Suddenly, Elliott realises that her path to happiness is littered with obstacles. Sci-fi comedy, starring Maisy Stella and Aubrey Plaza

programme.title: Hunting the Rolex Rippers
programme.desc: The search for the gang of thieves known for stalking celebrities on social media and targeting those with expensive watches. Journalist Tir Dhondy gets access, for the first time ever, to the gang members behind the crime. She meets the teenage moped riders snatching watches off the street, as well as a gang leader who runs a team of teenage watch rippers and female watch spotters. Tir also comes face-to-face with the 16-year-old who used a machete to steal singer Aled Jones' £17,000 Rolex in broad daylight

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Part one of two. Tir Dhondy reveals the realities behind modern Las Vegas. The first episode explores the risk of crime faced by tourists and visits a pool party where massive screens show scores of live sports events from around the world so that partygoers can bet from the comfort of their sun lounger

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tir Dhondy goes further into the underbelly of Las Vegas to investigate how the reinvention of the city to pull in a new generation of visitors is affecting locals

programme.title: Pobl Bachyn
programme.desc: When Adam goes to visit his friends Ed and Kath after a break-up, he is hoping for some consolation. Unbeknownst to him, he arrived during Pobl Bachyn, a long-standing tradition in the town of Twll involving folkloric hookmen coming after house guests in the middle of the night

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall takes a helicopter trip over Brazil's northern savannah in search of the giant anteater and encounters an unusual owl

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall continues his journey in the rainforests of Brazil, where he searches for piranha and encounters one of the most venomous snakes in the world

programme.title: Horrible Histories: The Movie
programme.desc: Atti, a smart and quick-witted Roman teenager, manages to upset Emperor Nero with one of his schemes. For punishment, Atti is sent to work in a cold and wet Britain, where he also meets the Celts. Family comedy, starring Sebastian Croft and Craig Roberts

programme.title: Ludwig
programme.desc: While investigating the suspicious death of a headmaster, John faces his bad school memories but is pleasantly surprised when he reconnects with his old mentor. Lucy follows a new lead in her search for James as her covert mission takes her to Wales

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge, based on her one-woman stage show about a woman wrestling with the demands of modern living through a mixture of bravado and casual sex. Fleabag struggles to make ends meet in the frenetic world of contemporary London as she tries to work out what she should be doing with her life. With Olivia Colman and Hugh Dennis

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Haunted by memories of her dead best friend, dumped by her emotionally fragile boyfriend, and now desperately trying to sell her stolen goods, Fleabag attempts to rekindle romantic fires to distract her from the mayhem of her life. Comedy, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Claire organises a surprise birthday party for herself. Fleabag helps her 'inappropriate' brother-in-law to shop for the perfect present, and runs into an old friend while trying to find a date. Award-winning comedy, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Claire takes Fleabag to an all-female silent retreat. What should be a period of peaceful solitude is disrupted by some explosive secrets coming to light, a surprising connection with an old acquaintance, and an unusual neighbouring workshop. Comedy, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Claire and Fleabag return to their father's home for an annual family gathering to mark the anniversary of their mother's death - but this time there is an unwanted guest. Fleabag gets into a confrontation with her godmother, who reveals her plans for a sexually charged art exhibition. Comedy, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Surprises are in store when Fleabag attends her godmother's exhibition of erotic art, having managed to acquire a date for the occasion. She finds herself confronted with a declaration of love and a very familiar piece of work, but Claire is notable by her absence. The awkward artistic gathering forces Fleabag to face up to the consequences of her actions - as well as finally revealing what happened to her best friend Boo. Conclusion of the comedy series, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Moz takes an interest in his new neighbour, while Nicki worries about the identity of her baby's father. Derrick and Yasuko are getting married, so Cartoon Head organises a stag night that degenerates into a full-blown riot. Comedy, starring Johnny Vegas

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Moz wakes up next to someone unexpected, before finding 200 pairs of shoes in his living room and accidentally filling his kitchen with foam. The day goes from bad to worse when his landlord announces he's paying a visit. Comedy, starring Johnny Vegas

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Moz wrestles with his confused feelings for Judith, but soon has other problems to contend with. PC impounds a crop of marijuana plants and needs somewhere to hide them, while suspicions arise that the deceased Craig's ghost is haunting the flat. Comedy, starring Johnny Vegas

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Elderly neighbour Mrs Coneybear is disturbed by strange noises and decides to hold a seance. Moz is concerned about keeping his marijuana plants a secret, Steve discovers what happened to his brother and Judith makes a surprising confession. Johnny Vegas stars

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Cartoon Head and Psycho Paul pressure Moz into helping them carry out a kidnapping, but he refuses - until the mouse-masked hit-man's infamous boss Stemroach pays him a visit. A policeman gets trapped in the loft, threatening not only the abduction but also the drug dealer's own business

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: Two emotionally unassembled young men start a play group, Funboys Fun Club, for childish young men to engage in some wholesome mucking about. When they come across severely depressed 60-year-old Marty, they try their best to bring some joy back to his life. Pilot for the sitcom, with Rian Lennon and Ryan Early

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter P, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Christine Walkden takes to the skies above Gloucestershire to pay visits to the gardens at Hidcote Manor and Kiftsgate Court. She cuts an underperforming shrub down to size, tackles an enormous rambling rose, and discovers the arts and crafts heritage of the Cotswolds

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Next stop on Anita Rani's east coast road trip is West Mersea in Essex, where oysters have been harvested for more than 2,000 years, cultivated in the creeks around Mersea Island. Anita heads onto the brackish waters to meet the people farming oysters and talks to a team investigating how the non-native Atlantic rock oyster is affecting the local ecosystem, and whether it could play a role in helping coastal habitats respond to rising sea levels. Along the way, Anita looks back through the Countryfile archives at the people and projects shaping life around the coastline

programme.title: Best of Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway goes island hopping as he visits Caldey off the Pembrokeshire coast, then Anglesey - known for its seabirds - before finishing in south Wales at Barry Island, his childhood home

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Ruth Vichos is in her vertical garden where she has strawberries, blossoms and various climbing varieties all demonstrating what you can be produced with stacking systems and plants that will follow vertical surfaces. Brian Cunningham reviews the wild flower beds he sowed in April and which will become a great resource for pollinators and the rest of the garden's ecosystem

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt celebrates the best of seasonal produce with the help of some favourite Saturday Kitchen chefs, with recipes by Bryn Williams, Poppy O'Toole and Gennaro Contaldo

programme.title: A Man for All Seasons
programme.desc: Henry VIII's decision to divorce Catherine of Aragon leads to a clash with the Roman Catholic Church and his own lord chancellor Sir Thomas More. To force his will, the king declares himself the supreme head of the Church of England and demands More's approval - but when he fails to get it, he puts his former adviser on trial for his life. Oscar-winning adaptation of Robert Bolt's history play, with Paul Scofield, Robert Shaw, Wendy Hiller, Leo McKern, John Hurt and Orson Welles

programme.title: I Was Monty's Double
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about actor and army officer ME Clifton-James, whose remarkable resemblance to General Montgomery saw him selected for a special assignment during the Second World War. He was trained to imitate the general perfectly, and sent on a tour of North Africa to keep the real Montgomery's whereabouts unknown to the enemy. Clifton-James stars as both himself and Monty, alongside John Mills and Cecil Parker

programme.title: All-Ireland Senior Hurling Championship Final
programme.desc: Galway v Limerick (Kick-off 3.30pm). Holly Hamilton presents all the action from the All-Ireland Senior Hurling Championship final at Croke Park. Limerick defeated Clare in the semi-finals and have already won the Allianz Hurling League this year, while Galway claimed a comfortable victory over the runners-up in that competition, Cork, in the last four. With analysis from Paul Murphy, Séamus Flanagan and Neil McManus, pitchside reports by Mark Sidebottom, and commentary from Thomas Niblock and Jamesie O'Connor

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and experts Mark Stacey and Philip Serrell are in Greater Manchester, where over 800 people arrived to have their items valued at Stockport Town Hall. Mark is impressed by a William De Morgan ceramic tile, Paul finds some etchings by artist John Buckland Wright and Philip discovers a gold necklace that surprises everyone at auction

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and art expert Philip Mould investigate the complex and controversial stories surrounding the origins of supposedly valuable works of art from around the world, discovering how to identify the handiwork of master forgers and revealing the bitter battles that occur in the seemingly genteel art world. In this episode, Fiona and Philip unravel the mystery of a suspected Fabergé flower, kept hidden in a shoebox in Liverpool but believed to have belonged to royalty - and worth a small fortune!

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the grounds of Wollaton Hall in Nottingham, where treasures include a fire extinguisher cocktail shaker, a toy monkey popping out of a musical pineapple, and underwear belonging to Queen Victoria. Fiona also learns about local activist George Powe, who campaigned to secure employment opportunities for black workers at Raleigh in the 1950s, and guesses the value of three cameo items

programme.title: The Guernsey Literary and Potato Peel Pie Society
programme.desc: In London, 1946, author Juliet Ashton's career has taken off but she is not enjoying promoting her detective stories to eager audiences on a book tour. When she receives a letter from a Guernsey pig farmer inviting her to attend a meeting of his literary society, she eagerly accepts. What Juliet discovers about her new friends and their secrets leads to her staying on the island much longer than she planned. Drama, starring Lily James, Tom Courtenay and Katherine Parkinson

programme.title: Golf: The Open Highlights
programme.desc: Action from day four of the fourth Major of the year, which took place at Royal Birkdale Golf Club in Southport, Merseyside. Scottie Scheffler secured the Claret Jug with a three-under-par 68 in the final round at Royal Portrush Golf Club last year, ensuring he scored a sub-70 round on all four days of the tournament, and finished four strokes ahead of his nearest competitor, fellow American Harris English

programme.title: ODI Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: England v India. Action from the third and final ODI of the series, which came from Lord's Cricket Ground in London

programme.title: Margrete: Queen of the North
programme.desc: In 1402, Queen Margrete has achieved what no one has managed before, gathering Denmark, Norway and Sweden into a peace-oriented union that she rules through her adopted son King Erik. But a conspiracy is in the making, and she finds herself in an impossible dilemma that could shatter her life's work. Period drama, starring Trine Dyrholm and Morten Hee Andersen. In Danish

programme.title: The Tech Billionaire Takeover
programme.desc: A new generation of tech billionaires believes its technologies can replace democracy and upend the global financial system. Matt Shea gets inside access to this new super-elite, visiting the new sovereign nations they are building and tracing their growing political connections to the Trump administration, aslking whether these men are trying to take over the world

programme.title: Ann Droid
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Diane Morgan and Sue Johnston. Sue's husband Dave has died, and her son is moving out to fix his marriage, so he decides to buy his mum a surprise - a robot care provider with a difference to help her live independently

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: The keepers face a new challenge as maned wolves arrive at the park for the first time. Native to South America, the three females are destined to share a habitat with capybara and tapir. But before any introductions can take place, the team must discover how the wolves will react to the scent of their future neighbours. Will they accept them as housemates or see them as something else entirely?

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Briony Harford and Lauren Wood help Emma from Mansfield downsize a home full of memories after losing her husband Simon. Finding items full of stories in his home library, the family are determined to turn his loss into legacy. Briony discovers an astonishing mix of collectibles that speak to Simon's curiosity while Lauren takes on a series of delicate and technically demanding restorations

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dean Westmoreland is presented with pair of pointe shoes worn by former ballerina Julie Felix for her landmark performance at the Royal Opera House, and Charlotte Abbott fixes a model Range Rover that represents a big globetrotting adventure by a six-man military team. Chris Shaw takes on a fragile 1917 scrapbook documenting the rise of the groundbreaking Dick Kerr Ladies, the munitions factory football team who became global pioneers of the women's game

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets a professional forager who takes him on a tour around the beautiful Camel Estuary. Inspired, the chef creates his version of a Greek Horta pie with foraged greens

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Four hippos take a huge step into their new home, while Ben Fogle and Kate Humble revisit the unforgettable story of beloved tiger Kadu

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: The case of a missing woman whose body was found buried in her own back garden, prompting her family and friends to raise suspicions about her boyfriend. Police needed hard evidence to prove his guilt - which they eventually got in the shape of incriminating CCTV footage

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: A forensic archaeologist helps determine if a woman's death at her husband's hands was premeditated, and a cell site analyst follows a phone trail that convicts bank robbers

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A former chapel in rural Hethersgill in Cumbria is converted into a glorious holiday home, while developers in Southall, west London and South Norwood, south London create lovely homes from worn-out property auction buys. Presented by Martel Maxwell and Owain Wyn Evans

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian learns about the history of door knockers at Wetherby Racecourse antiques fair. Philip Serrell and Charles Hanson help two couples to find a profitable purchase

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kemah Bob, JJ Chalmers, Kevin Clifton and Victoria Derbyshire go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host this quiz contest, in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try and win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: For Sharon and Andrew, showing their Coloured Ryeland sheep at the Mid Devon Show is a family affair. Gillian and Ian have brought theirs too, with tears as the results roll in

programme.title: The Hidden World of Hospitality with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The changeable UK weather can bring sharp peaks and troughs in revenue for hospitality businesses. Tom heads to a remote hotel in Scotland, an ice cream parlour in north Wales and his own restaurant in the heart of London to look at inventive methods businesses employ to cope with fluctuating seasonal demand

programme.title: Wild Brazil
programme.desc: Stephen Mangan narrates a documentary following capuchin monkeys, giant otters and coatis as they strive to raise their families. The first episode sees the young animals taking their first steps in some of the world's most extraordinary habitats, where they have to beware of deadly predators including the jaguar and caiman

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: James Lewis and Nick Davies examine antiques brought to them at the Milestones Museum in Basingstoke, Hampshire, while Paul Martin finds out about the town's industrial heritage

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Richard invites back Fern Britton, JB Gill, John Kearns and Sally Lindsay - former players who came close to winning last time round - for another bite at the cherry

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former players Fern Britton, JB Gill, John Kearns and Sally Lindsay test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: In the aftermath of a political earthquake in Hungary, Stephanie Hegarty explores whether the downfall of Viktor Orbán could be a warning sign for populist leaders everywhere. Plus, the programme investigates the Russian nationalists who are taking the law into their own hands

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking questions on specialist subjects of British and Irish cheeses, Roman general Sulla, TV series Jonathan Creek, and composer Johannes Brahms, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as sci-fi fans Two Hearts take on science enthusiasts Ad Astra. The teams must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues comprising question hard, comically criticise, illegally hunt, and pus-filled swelling

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Amol Rajan asks the questions in the second of the academic quiz's first-round matches, with four students from Durham University pitting their wits against a team from the University of Manchester in a bid to reach the second stage of the competition

programme.title: Evolution
programme.desc: Chris Packham tells the story of how the ostrich came to lay the biggest egg on Earth, exploring the way reproduction has shaped life and the surprising evolutionary origins of sex, eggs and birds

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob are on the River Severn in Worcestershire to fish for zander, and enlist the help of local guide Tim Maslen to find the best spots. As they sit and wait, they ruminate on the state of Britain's rivers in general, the threats that they are under and what it takes to do something to help them improve. After a few hours they move down the river to a secluded beach and meet their special guest - local actor and self-confessed fishing addict Charlie Cooper, star of This Country

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day, presented by Katie Razzall

programme.title: In the Heat of the Night
programme.desc: When a wealthy industrialist is murdered in Mississippi, the bigoted local sheriff is quick to accuse a black newcomer to town - only to learn his suspect is a highly respected homicide detective from Philadelphia. However, the duo are forced to put aside their mutual loathing and team up to track down the killer. Oscar-winning drama, starring Sidney Poitier, Rod Steiger and Warren Oates

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Next stop on Anita Rani's east coast road trip is West Mersea in Essex, where oysters have been harvested for more than 2,000 years, cultivated in the creeks around Mersea Island. Anita heads onto the brackish waters to meet the people farming oysters and talks to a team investigating how the non-native Atlantic rock oyster is affecting the local ecosystem, and whether it could play a role in helping coastal habitats respond to rising sea levels. Along the way, Anita looks back through the Countryfile archives at the people and projects shaping life around the coastline

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Host Vernon Kay and unreliable tour guide Paul Gorton test Susan Calman, Max Fosh and Noreen Khan on their knowledge of the UK, beginning with Dundee

programme.title: Scamanda
programme.desc: When an investigative reporter starts digging into Amanda's story, she finds that things are not adding up. Amanda continues to blog and fundraise - but soon, someone else joins the investigation

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In a Northern Ireland homecoming in County Down, Jules Hudson helps golf-loving Siobhan find a countryside home for her family but not without some sisterly support

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: The daughter of a footballer who played for Wolverhampton Wanderers in the 1940s seeks JJ Chalmers and James Broad's help finding new homes for her parents' belongings

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Comedian and actress Sophie Willan takes over as host, welcoming 12 contestants into the sewing room, with judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young setting three tasks on the theme of the British summer. The pattern challenge sees them crafting summer shorts with a patch pocket, before they have to make barbecue aprons into bags in the transformation challenge. Finally, the sewers are asked to create garments to fit themselves as they make outfits for their ideal summer's day and model them on the catwalk

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kemah Bob, JJ Chalmers, Kevin Clifton and Victoria Derbyshire test their general knowledge skills in the hope of winning a prize

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host this quiz contest, in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try and win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: The team combines sustainability with staying on trend, with Kirsty Wilson demonstrating how to create a living herb wall made out of recycled materials

programme.title: The Hundred
programme.desc: MI London Women v Sunrisers Leeds Women (Start-time 2.45pm). Coverage of the match from The Kia Oval in London, as the two teams get the season under way. The Sunrisers won last year's competition thanks to a seven-wicket victory against Southern Brave at Lord's. The Brave made 115-6 in their 100 balls before the Sunrisers, previously known as Northern Superchargers last year, chased down their target with 12 balls to spare

programme.title: The Hundred
programme.desc: MI London Men v Sunrisers Leeds Men (Start-time 6.30pm). Coverage of the match from The Kia Oval in London. The hosts, who were known as Oval Invincibles last season, won the final last year thanks to an impressive 26-run victory over Trent Rockets. They made 168-5 in their 100 balls with Will Jacks top-scoring with 72 from just 41 balls, before they restricted the Rockets to 142-8 in their reply

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig offers quite interesting facts about thrills and spills with Nish Kumar, Mark Steel, Suzi Ruffell joining regular panellist Alan Davies

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Jen Brister plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing routines by fellow comedians Esther Manito and Adam Rowe

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Victoria Derbyshire

programme.title: The History Boys
programme.desc: Eight unruly grammar school boys preparing to take their Oxbridge entrance exams are exposed to the contrasting teaching styles of two very different schoolmasters - the intellectually enthusiastic Hector, who seeks to instil a love of knowledge for its own sake, and the more cynical new arrival Irwin, who intends to equip them with the specific information they need to get ahead. Comedy drama adapted from Alan Bennett's play, with Richard Griffiths, Stephen Campbell Moore and Frances de la Tour

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie joins the St John Ambulance team at a busy county show where a boy requires urgent help, and takes a placement at a centre supporting veterans adjusting to life beyond the forces. Back in college, Anna's physio class pushes her in a different way

programme.title: My Epic Camel Adventure with Gordon Buchanan
programme.desc: The wildlife cameraman takes a ride through the Gobi desert, starting in the west of Mongolia and crossing blistering sand dunes, endless rocky plains and the treacherous Bumbat Mountain with only his camels for company. To succeed he must befriend the animals, learn as much as he can about them, and try to win their trust and respect, discovering how these creatures have evolved in the most unusual ways to survive and prosper in one of the most hostile environments on the planet

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps house hunter Andrew swap city life in Amsterdam for a characterful cottage in the Peak District for him and his faitful dog Edna. Away from the search, Sonali also discovers how local woodlands are being transformed, as young native trees are creating a greener future following the impact of ash dieback

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Robin Johnson are in the Scottish village of East Linton to help lawyer-turned-university worker Anne declutter her crammed flat to raise funds for a campervan adventure. But can a dishevelled Hindu deity bring them the fortunes they need?

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Tokyo on a sleek Shinkansen bullet train and explores the ornate Akasaka Palace, built in 1909 by Emperor Meiji for his son, the crown prince Yoshihito. On board a sleek Shinkansen bullet train, Michael arrives in Tokyo, one of the greatest cities on earth, with a population of 37 million in the metropolitan area. He begins by exploring the ornate Akasaka Palace, built in 1909 by Emperor Meiji for his son, the crown prince Yoshihito. He's surprised to find that, with its neo-baroque architecture and impressive fountains, it looks more than a little British. In the fashionable district of Ginza, Michael journeys underground to ride the metro, where he is intrigued by the catchy jingles played at each station and meets one of the composers behind 300 of the tunes

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on an adventure through the western Mediterranean islands of Sardinia and Corsica, beginning in Sardinia's capital of Cagliari. He delves into the city's Roman past and experiences warm hospitality with a taste of the traditional myrtle liqueur, and discovers the legacy of Eleanora de Arborea, the pioneering medieval ruler who shaped Sardinia's laws

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kemah Bob, JJ Chalmers, Kevin Clifton and Victoria Derbyshire test their general knowledge skills in the hope of winning a prize

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host this quiz contest, in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try and win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: The Hundred
programme.desc: Southern Brave Women v Welsh Fire Women (Start-time 3.00pm). Coverage of the match from Utilita Bowl in Southampton. The Brave finished top of the table last season after winning all eight of their group matches, but they were beaten in the final by Northern Superchargers. Welsh Fire didn't fare so well last year as they finished bottom of the table after only picking up one victory in their eight matches

programme.title: The Hundred
programme.desc: Southern Brave Men v Welsh Fire Men (Start-time 6.30pm). Coverage of the match from Utilita Bowl in Southampton. The hosts missed out on finals day last year after they finished in fourth place with four wins from their eight games. Welsh Fire struggled last season as they ended their campaign bottom of the table after they only picked up two victories from their eight matches

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: With Storm Eunice, and its 100 mph winds, battering the south coast, the Portsmouth crew have to decide whether they can even launch to a windsurfer in trouble. Once they're on the water, the situation escalates when it's reported that the casualty doesn't have his diabetes medication with him. In Skerries, on the east coast of Ireland, a husband watches on as his wife struggles on her kayak. When the crew race to her rescue, they discover her in the water and close to exhaustion

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Victoria Derbyshire

programme.title: Uncanny
programme.desc: Adaptation of the Radio 4 series, featuring real-life accounts of supernatural encounters, expert analysis, and investigations. Presented by Danny Robins

programme.title: Amityville: An Origin Story
programme.desc: The Lutz family turn their story into book and film, after paranormal trailblazers Ed and Lorraine Warren confirm a demonic presence at 112 Ocean Ave. However, their fame comes at a cost when they are accused of perpetrating a massive hoax

programme.title: Amityville: An Origin Story
programme.desc: The haunted house is reinvented by the success of The Amityville Horror, inspiring the creation of sequels, conspiracy theories, and rabid fandom. Plus, George Lutz's secret past is revealed and the absurd legacy of Amityville comes into focus

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Four hippos take a huge step into their new home, while Ben Fogle and Kate Humble revisit the unforgettable story of beloved tiger Kadu

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: James Broad and Lauren Wood help dog groomer Tanya to let go of her parents' treasured items to fund a long-dreamed-of trip to India, honouring her father's memory and reconnecting with her heritage

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don keeps the garden thriving through summer by cutting back perennials, managing long grass, pruning apples and sowing vegetables, before harvesting new potatoes and sweet peas. Adam Frost visits a remarkable Surrey walled garden, while Sue Kent shares creative growing ideas, and experts explore healthier crops and beautiful crassulas

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.desc: Free-range pig farming couple SJ and Nat, along with organic cattle farmers Emma and Ian, try to sell their home-grown food at the Lancashire Game and Country Festival

programme.title: The Hidden World of Hospitality with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Transforming a casual dining concept into a chain of restaurants is one of the most high-risk ventures in hospitality. The chef goes behind the scenes at two restaurants looking to expand their businesses. They include Harvest, a new fusion restaurant right next to Windsor Castle. Tom also shares his own plans regarding The Butcher's Tap and Grill

programme.title: Wild Brazil
programme.desc: The animal families face the formidable monsoon rains and the giant otter cubs must watch out for predatory jaguars and learn to swim before their river floods. The coatis have to eat as much as they can ahead of their habitat disappearing underwater, while the youngest capuchin monkey needs to master some clever survival skills. Stephen Mangan narrates

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Norwich Cathedral, once home to Benedictine monks. Experts Thomas Plant and David Fletcher pick out some items to take to auction. Among them are an old astronomical map and a watercolour by a local artist. Paul also explores the history of the nearby Broads

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former players Fern Britton, JB Gill, John Kearns and Sally Lindsay test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former players Fern Britton, JB Gill, John Kearns and Sally Lindsay test their general knowledge skills in the penultimate round of challenges

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Derbyshire with a recently married couple who are making an epic location shift and swapping the Blue Mountains in Australia for the wilds of the Peak District. They have a significant budget of £750,000 and have their hearts set on a character home with a large garden for their three dogs. Away from his quest, Jules comes across an unlikely centre of manufacturing nestled in the county's rural countryside

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts joins archaeologists on digs of northern Britain, revealing a Roman Emperor's lost bath house, the sunken treasures of medieval pilgrims and a formidable fortress perched on top of a Scottish mountain

programme.title: The Devil in the Family: The Fall of Ruby Franke
programme.desc: When popular vlogger Ruby Franke was arrested for child abuse in August 2023, it quickly became one of the biggest trending stories in America. For years, the Frankes' YouTube channel had documented a wholesome, happy family life, with nearly three million subscribers tuning in at its peak. But happy families are rarely what they seem. As the cracks began to show, the family turned to life coach Jodi Hildebrandt for guidance - and rapidly found themselves trapped in a nightmare

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Lawrence arranges a special reunion on a boat for his old university friends Darren and Callum, but the day does not unfold quite as they expected. As they strike out across the remote lake, their conversation takes them into troubled waters. Darkly comic anthology, written by and starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Mark Gatiss and Diane Morgan

programme.title: The North York Moors: A Wild Year
programme.desc: The red grouse of the North York Moors, which enjoy the shoots of the heather in spring, which is almost all that can grow on the sandstone of the moorlands

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events with Faisal Islam

programme.title: The Inspection
programme.desc: A gay black man enlists in the Marine Corps and ends up at boot camp on Parris Island, South Carolina. He initially meets the physical requirements, but is not as successful in disguising his sexual orientation, making him the target of a near-lethal hazing from training instructor Leland Laws and a fellow recruit. LGBTQ+ drama, starring Jeremy Pope, Gabrielle Union and Bokeem Woodbine

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Central Leeds crew Nagina and Mo are called to a siege where police are currently negotiating with a man who is threatening to harm himself and the officers attending. They must be ready to enter the building to treat any potential casualties. Meanwhile, married crewmates Jen and Elliott are dispatched to a 61-year-old in Yeadon, who has had a suspected stroke, then treat a patient having a seizure at the train station

programme.title: Evolution
programme.desc: Chris Packham tells the story of how life on Earth has developed over more than four billion years, charting the changes that gave rise to five modern animals, beginning with the African elephant

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Two young Amur tigers prepare to leave home as part of an international breeding programme to help secure the future of one of the world's rarest big cats. Ben Fogle and Kate Humble revisit an extraordinary conservation adventure when Longleat keepers travelled to Tanzania to help conservationist Tony Fitzjohn relocate endangered African wild dogs

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: A young family hope their 1980s classic toy will click with the dealers, an intriguing seat leads to a guessing game, and Nigel Havers is enchanted by a piece of London history
