programme.title: Caban Banana Gareth
programme.desc: Gareth the Orangutan visits schools across Wales and questions youngsters about their favourite topics

programme.title: Fferm Fach
programme.desc: Cai goes on an adventure with Hywel the magical farmer to Fferm Fach to see how rhubarb is grown

programme.title: Blwi
programme.desc: Animated children's show following a dog who is bursting with energy, imagination and curiosity

programme.title: Parc Glan Gwil
programme.desc: It is time for Parc Glan Gwil's flower competition, but Syr Gwil notices that there is a big issue with the flowers this year. With the crew's help, the park looks its best once again before Blodwen Jones arrives to judge the competition

programme.title: Ein Byd Bach Ni
programme.desc: A visit to Canada, witnessing the Niagra Falls, the capital city Ottawa and learning about Canadian symbols such as the maple leaf

programme.title: Ahoi!
programme.desc: Pupils from Ysgol Llwyncelyn join Ben Dant and Cadi for an exciting adventure

programme.title: Sam Tan
programme.desc: Sam and Siarlys have gone away for a quite weekend to Pontypandy island, but the experience doesn't turn out to be all that quiet

programme.title: Awyr Iach
programme.desc: An action-packed outdoor adventure series

programme.title: Blero yn Mynd i Ocido
programme.desc: Children's programme

programme.title: Byd Tad-Cu
programme.desc: A man tells tall tales to his grandchildren when they ask him questions, but Halifax the rabbit always gives the real answer. Don Warrington stars

programme.title: Crawc a'i Ffrindiau
programme.desc: Animation based on The Wind in the Willows stories

programme.title: Annibendod
programme.desc: Mrs Moss has reached the finals of the Best Welsh Head prize, and Miss Enfys asks Anni and her friends in the After School Club to make arrangements for the celebration party

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Animated adventures of a truck and his best pal as they try to help their friends in the little town of Swoppiton

programme.title: Dal Dy Ddannedd
programme.desc: Teams from Ysgol Y Fenni join Pwdryn and Melys to play a series of candyland-themed games. The challenge is to collect as many clean teeth as possible and win the Dani Dant Trophy

programme.title: Penblwyddi Cyw
programme.desc: A look back at some of this week's birthday greetings

programme.title: Y Cosmos
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on how light escapes from the sun, following its creation from nuclear fusion, its battle against gravity and magnetism, and its leap into space

programme.title: Gwesty Aduniad
programme.desc: Ann from Penllyn gets an answer she has been after for 70 years. Miss Wales meets someone from her past and Richard from Essex meets a family from Wales who changed his life

programme.title: Garddio a Mwy
programme.desc: Gardening and nature

programme.title: Gerddi Cymru
programme.desc: Aled Samuel visits the Vale of Glamorgan to see the lavish gardens at Dyffryn and the small and perfectly formed Cowbridge Physic Garden

programme.title: Cyfres Triathlon Cymru 2026
programme.desc: Highlights of the third leg of the Welsh Triathlon Series from the town of Llandudno. Presented by Lowri Morgan and Gareth Roberts

programme.title: Yr Afon
programme.desc: Iolo Williams travels along the Amazon, witnessing the commercial developments responsible for the decline of the wilderness protecting the river, which faces an uncertain future

programme.title: Iaith ar Daith
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley is accompanied by broadcaster and presenter Jason Mohammad as she embarks on a linguistic road trip across Wales. Kate will attempt to learn Welsh and will face a variety of challenges along the way, with Jason will by her side throughout

programme.title: Priodas Pum Mil
programme.desc: Trystan Ellis-Morris and Emma Walford set about organising the wedding of Einir and Jason in Dyffryn Nantlle

programme.title: Arfordir Cymru: Sir Benfro
programme.desc: Bedwyr Rees travels from Newport in Pembrokeshire to Fishguard, meeting a sculptor who is inspired by names along the coastline and learning about a project to safeguard the unique coastline

programme.title: Wil, Aeron a'r Inca
programme.desc: Wil Hendreseifion and Aeron Pughe travel to the highest peaks of Peru, where they live and work alongside a community of shepherds in the Andes, learning about the farming traditions of the region, which date back to the Inca civilisation

programme.title: Cefn Gwlad
programme.desc: A profile of Gethin Havard, Honorary President of this year's Royal Welsh Show. Ifan Jones Evans visits Sennybridge to marvel at an area rich in agricultural land but whose people have had to fight for the right to farm their land. Here, at the foot of the MOD-owned Epynt mountain range is where Gethin has lived and farmed all of his life, with the history of the surrounding area deeply engrained in the blood of this highly respected stockman

programme.title: Pobol y Cwm Omnibws
programme.desc: Omnibus edition looking at the events in Cwmderi over the past week. With English subtitles

programme.title: Y Wal
programme.desc: Lisa Jen travels to Ceuta to meet people trying to cross the border from Africa into Europe

programme.title: Y Sioe 2026
programme.desc: Mari Lovgreen and Ifan Jones Evans look forward to the Royal Welsh Show and enjoy the 'Moliant y Maes' service from the showground in Builth Wells led by this year's feature county, Brecknockshire

programme.title: Seiclo: Tour de France
programme.desc: Action from the 15th stage of the Grand Tour race, a 183.9km mountain route from Champagnole to Plateau de Solaison

programme.title: Tabw
programme.desc: Jess Davies explores lip fillers and asks why the cosmetic industry in Wales is so unregulated

programme.title: Cymoedd Roy Noble
programme.desc: Roy visits one of the valleys in Blaenau Gwent, exploring the regenerated site of the old steel works at Ebbw Vale. He meets the sister of the late actor Victor Spinetti in Cwm before moving on to the village of Six Bells near Abertillery, and Nantyglo. His journey ends in Brynmawr, where he attends an outdoor screening at the town's Market Hall Cinema

programme.title: Blociau Rhif
programme.desc: Fun and games for young children with the number blocks

programme.title: Sam Tan
programme.desc: Hanna, James and Sara launch a space sandwich amidst some disagreement. When the space sandwich camera spots a distress flare they call Fireman Sam and his crew to save the day

programme.title: Blwi

programme.desc: The youngster makes a mistake in his drawing and colours in his shorts the wrong colour, so he decides not to draw himself at all. But his friends in the Art World want to find him

programme.desc: The sign in the gymnastics club is broken and Harri needs to go over to help Cadi fix it. However, Harri decides it would be better to make a new sign, so goes off to buy paint, making a big mess at the same time

programme.title: Prosiect Z
programme.desc: The Zeds arrive in Ysgol y Preseli. It remains to be seen whether they will escape or be turned into Zeds

programme.title: Garddio a Mwy
programme.desc: In this year's Eisteddfod area, the team explores gardens, the coast and a local planting project

programme.title: Seiclo: Tour de France
programme.desc: Coverage of the 20th and penultimate stage of the Grand Tour race, a 170.9km mountain route from Le Bourg d'Oisans to Alpe d'Huez

programme.title: Cymry ar Gynfas
programme.desc: Caernarfon-based artist Steve 'Pablo' Jones packs a bag and heads to Covent Garden in London, to St Paul's Church to meet his muse, singer and presenter Aled Jones. Though Aled and Steve do seem like the odd couple, they hit it off straight away and were soon lost in conversation like a couple of old friends. Aled feels able to open up about the time when he lost the love for singing, and Steve hopes to be able to capture a sense of that vulnerability when it comes to the final portrait

programme.title: Cynefin
programme.desc: The team discovers the history of the border town of Newtown or Drenewydd in Powys. Heledd Cynwal is in awe of the architecture at Gregynog, Iestyn Jones has a dip in the River Severn and hears about ambitious plans to expand the town, Llinos Owen tells the story of the textiles industry in the town, and Sion Tomos Owen follows in the footsteps of some of the areas more colourful characters

programme.title: Live Major League Cricket
programme.desc: Coverage of the final, which takes place at Oakland Coliseum in California. Last year's showpiece saw MI New York go home with the trophy thanks to a thrilling five-run victory. They made 180-7 in their 20 overs with Quinton de Kock hitting 77 from just 46 balls. In reply Washington Freedom were restricted to 175-5, and despite Rachin Ravindra hitting 70 from 41 balls, they fell just short of their target

programme.desc: England v India. Coverage of the third and final ODI of the series from Lord's Cricket Ground in London. The hosts are looking for their first series victory in this format since a 2-1 win at home in 2018. Since then India have won three series on the trot, as well as coming out on top in seven of the last nine meetings between the two sides

programme.title: The Hundred Live
programme.desc: MI London Women v Sunrisers Leeds Women (Start-time 2.45pm). Coverage of the match from The Kia Oval in London, as the two teams get the season underway. The Sunrisers won last year's competition thanks to a seven-wicket victory against Southern Brave at Lord's. The Brave made 115-6 in their 100 balls before the Sunrisers, previously known as Northern Superchargers last year, chased down their target with 12 balls to spare

programme.title: The Hundred Live
programme.desc: MI London Men v Sunrisers Leeds Men (Start-time 6.30pm). Coverage of the match from The Kia Oval in London. The hosts, who were known as Oval Invincibles last season, won the final last year thanks to an impressive 26-run victory over Trent Rockets. They made 168-5 in their 100 balls with Will Jacks top-scoring with 72 from just 41 balls, before they restricted the Rockets to 142-8 in their reply

programme.desc: London Spirit Women v Manchester Super Giants Women (Start-time 3.00pm). Coverage of the match from Lord's in London. programme.title: The Hundred Live
programme.desc: London Spirit Men v Manchester Super Giants Men (Start-time 6.30pm). Coverage of the match from Lord's in London. programme.title: The Hundred Live
programme.desc: Sunrisers Leeds Women v Southern Brave Women (Start-time 11.00am). Coverage of the match at Headingley Cricket Ground. The Sunrisers, who were known as the Northern Superchargers last year won the competition in 2025 after they defeated the Brave by seven wickets in the final. The visitors had finished top of the table 12 months ago but the hosts dominated the final as they chased down their target of 116 with 12 balls to spare

programme.title: The Hundred Live
programme.desc: Sunrisers Leeds Men v Southern Brave Men (Start-time 2.30pm). Coverage of the match at Headingley Cricket Ground. The hosts won this meeting last season thanks to a three-wicket victory in Southampton. The Brave hit 139-5 in their 100 balls before the hosts, known then as the Northern Superchargers, won the game off the very last ball of the contest as Graham Clark led the way with 38 not out from 24 balls

programme.title: EFL Cup Retro
programme.desc: Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur in the 2008/09 final. Spurs were managed by Harry Redknapp and had a strong run that led them to the final of the competition, having beaten the likes of Newcastle, Liverpool and Watford in previous rounds. The Red Devils had beaten Middlesbrough, Queens Park Rangers and Blackburn Rovers in the early rounds, before beating Derby County 4-3 on aggregate

programme.title: The Open Live
programme.desc: Coverage of day four of the fourth Major of the year, which takes place at Royal Birkdale Golf Club in Southport, Merseyside. Scottie Scheffler secured the Claret Jug with a three-under-par 68 in the final round at Royal Portrush Golf Club last year, ensuring he scored a sub-70 round on all four days of the tournament, and finished four strokes ahead of his nearest competitor, fellow American Harris English. Matt Fitzpatrick was the best-placed homegrown player, finishing tied for fourth alongside Wyndham Clark and Li Haotong, while Rory McIlroy was tied for seventh place, seven strokes behind Scheffler

programme.title: Live Ladies European Tour Golf
programme.desc: The ISPS HANDA Women's Scottish Open. Coverage of the first day of the tournament, which takes place at Dundonald Links in Irvine, Ayrshire. This is the sixth consecutive year that this event has taken place at this venue, as England's Lottie Woad looks to defend the title that she won here last year. She picked up her first LPGA Tour victory 12 months ago when she finished three strokes clear of South Korea's Kim Hyo-joo

programme.title: Live European Seniors Tour Golf
programme.desc: The Senior Open Championship. Coverage of day one of the fifth Major tournament of the year, which takes place at Gleneagles in Auchterarder, Perthshire. Ireland's Pádraig Harrington is the reigning champion following his triumph at Sunningdale Golf Club in Berkshire 12 months ago, where he finished on an overall score of 16-under-par, three strokes ahead of Thomas Bjørn and Justin Leonard who tied for second place

programme.title: Live PGA Tour Golf
programme.desc: The 3M Open. Coverage of the featured groups on day one of the tournament, which takes place at TPC Twin Cities in Blaine, Minnesota

programme.title: Live PGA Tour Golf
programme.desc: The 3M Open. Further coverage of day one of the tournament, which takes place at TPC Twin Cities in Blaine, Minnesota. This is a relatively recent addition to the PGA circuit with the event first taking place in 2019. Six out of the seven winners of this tournament have come from the United States, with Kurt Kitayama winning here 12 months ago. The American ended on 23-under-par as he finished one stroke clear of nearest challenger Sam Stevens

programme.desc: The 3M Open. Coverage of the featured groups on day two of the tournament, which takes place at TPC Twin Cities in Blaine, Minnesota. Kurt Kitayama finished one stroke clear of his American compatriot Sam Stevens here last year to win his second title on the tour and will be looking to become the first-ever player to retain this title

programme.title: Live Ladies European Tour Golf
programme.desc: The ISPS HANDA Women's Scottish Open. Coverage of the third day of the tournament, which takes place at Dundonald Links in Irvine, Ayrshire. This is the sixth consecutive year that this event has taken place at this venue, as England's Lottie Woad looks to defend the title that she won here last year. She picked up her first LPGA Tour victory 12 months ago when she finished three strokes clear of South Korea's Kim Hyo-joo

programme.title: Live Major League Cricket
programme.desc: Coverage of the final, which takes place at Oakland Coliseum in California. Last year's showpiece saw MI New York go home with the trophy thanks to a thrilling five-run victory. They made 180-7 in their 20 overs with Quinton de Kock hitting 77 from just 46 balls. In reply Washington Freedom were restricted to 175-5, and despite Rachin Ravindra hitting 70 from 41 balls, they fell just short of their target

programme.title: World Cup Breakfast
programme.desc: Headlines and stories surrounding the FIFA World Cup

programme.title: Sunday at The Open
programme.desc: Build-up to the fourth and final day of the Major from Royal Birkdale Golf Club in Southport, Merseyside

programme.title: The Open Live
programme.desc: Coverage of day four of the fourth Major of the year, which takes place at Royal Birkdale Golf Club in Southport, Merseyside. Scottie Scheffler secured the Claret Jug with a three-under-par 68 in the final round at Royal Portrush Golf Club last year, ensuring he scored a sub-70 round on all four days of the tournament, and finished four strokes ahead of his nearest competitor, fellow American Harris English. Matt Fitzpatrick was the best-placed homegrown player, finishing tied for fourth alongside Wyndham Clark and Li Haotong, while Rory McIlroy was tied for seventh place, seven strokes behind Scheffler

programme.title: Live World Matchplay Darts
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day two of the tournament, which takes place at Winter Gardens in Blackpool, and features four first-round matches. Luke Littler impressed with a one-sided 10-2 triumph over Ryan Searle at this stage 12 months ago, while Gary Anderson also put in a stellar performance by beating Luke Woodhouse 10-5

programme.title: The Hundred Live
programme.desc: MI London Women v Sunrisers Leeds Women (Start-time 2.45pm). Coverage of the match from The Kia Oval in London, as the two teams get the season underway. The Sunrisers won last year's competition thanks to a seven-wicket victory against Southern Brave at Lord's. The Brave made 115-6 in their 100 balls before the Sunrisers, previously known as Northern Superchargers last year, chased down their target with 12 balls to spare

programme.title: The Hundred Live
programme.desc: MI London Men v Sunrisers Leeds Men (Start-time 6.30pm). Coverage of the match from The Kia Oval in London. The hosts, who were known as Oval Invincibles last season, won the final last year thanks to an impressive 26-run victory over Trent Rockets. They made 168-5 in their 100 balls with Will Jacks top-scoring with 72 from just 41 balls, before they restricted the Rockets to 142-8 in their reply

programme.title: Live World Matchplay Darts
programme.desc: Coverage of day four of the tournament, which takes place at Winter Gardens in Blackpool, and features four second-round matches. programme.title: The Hundred Live
programme.desc: MI London Women v Sunrisers Leeds Women (Start-time 2.45pm). Coverage of the match from The Kia Oval in London, as the two teams get the season underway. The Sunrisers won last year's competition thanks to a seven-wicket victory against Southern Brave at Lord's. The Brave made 115-6 in their 100 balls before the Sunrisers, previously known as Northern Superchargers last year, chased down their target with 12 balls to spare

programme.title: The Hundred Live
programme.desc: MI London Men v Sunrisers Leeds Men (Start-time 6.30pm). Coverage of the match from The Kia Oval in London. The hosts, who were known as Oval Invincibles last season, won the final last year thanks to an impressive 26-run victory over Trent Rockets. They made 168-5 in their 100 balls with Will Jacks top-scoring with 72 from just 41 balls, before they restricted the Rockets to 142-8 in their reply

programme.title: The Hundred Live
programme.desc: Southern Brave Women v Welsh Fire Women (Start-time 3.00pm). Coverage of the match from Utilita Bowl in Southampton. The Brave finished top of the table last season after winning all eight of their group matches, but they were beaten in the final by Northern Superchargers. Welsh Fire didn't fare so well last year as they finished bottom of the table after only picking up one victory in their eight matches

programme.title: The Hundred Live
programme.desc: Southern Brave Men v Welsh Fire Men (Start-time 6.30pm). Coverage of the match from Utilita Bowl in Southampton. The hosts missed out on finals day last year after they finished in fourth place with four wins from their eight games. Welsh Fire struggled last season as they ended their campaign bottom of the table after they only picked up two victories from their eight matches

programme.title: Live: One Day International Cricket
programme.desc: West Indies v New Zealand. Coverage of the fourth ODI in the five-match series from Kensington Oval in Barbados. The visitors are searching for their fourth consecutive ODI series victory against the West Indies, having won the previous three by scores of 3-0, 2-1 and 3-0. The hosts haven't beaten the Kiwis in a series in this format since 2012 when they won a five-match contest 4-1

programme.title: Live MLB
programme.desc: Philadelphia Phillies v Los Angeles Dodgers (Start-time 11.40pm). Coverage of the National League match from Citizens Bank Park. The Dodgers claimed a 2-1 series win the last time the two sides met in Los Angeles at the end of May. The Dodgers won the first game 4-2 before the Phillies fought back to win 4-3 in the second match. In the decider Los Angeles claimed an impressive 9-1 victory to win the series

programme.title: Live: Commonwealth Games 360
programme.desc: Coverage from the day session on day one of the Games, which take place in Glasgow. The games first took place back in Hamilton, Canada in 1930 and took place again in 1934 and 1938, before being cancelled in 1942 and 1946 because of the Second World War. Since its return in 1950 the games have continued to take place every four years and are being held in Glasgow for the second time in 12 years, having previously been held in the Scottish city in 2014. Events taking place today include swimming and para swimming, boxing, para powerlifting and artistic gymnastics

The Scottish city previously hosted these games back in 2014, with other British cities to host include Manchester, Edinburgh, Cardiff and London. Birmingham hosted the previous event in 2022 which saw a record-breaking 5054 competitors take part. Events taking place today include netball, boxing, artistic gymnastics, and bowls and para bowls

programme.title: The Mating Game
programme.desc: Series exploring the lengths that animals go to in search of a mate, featuring footage of dancing, fighting and even deceiving potential partners. The first episode features footage of a young ostrich preparing to mate for the first time and an ageing zebra fending off younger rivals, as well as the sneakier tactics of the nursery web spider and kangaroos fighting for supremacy. Plus, male birds that pose as females to get closer to their intended mates unnoticed

programme.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
programme.desc: With the web-slinger's identity now revealed to the world, Peter Parker asks Doctor Strange for magical help to undo the damage. However, a spell gone wrong unleashes dangerous foes from parallel universes on the world. Comic-book adventure sequel, starring Tom Holland, Zendaya and Benedict Cumberbatch, with Willem Dafoe, Jamie Foxx and Alfred Molina among the stars of past Spider-Man movies reprising their roles

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani continues her east coast road trip, visiting the Naze, a nature reserve on the Essex coast. She also joins a group of fossil hunters on the search for the secrets revealed by coastal erosion. Plus, reports from the archive exploring the hidden histories of the British landscape

programme.title: MOTD Live: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Spain v Argentina (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the final, which takes place at New York/New Jersey Stadium in the USA. Spain reached only their second World Cup final by defeating tournament favourites France with a masterclass in their semi-final, winning 2-0 and completely nullifying a disjointed French outfit with ease. They won on their only other appearance in the final, against the Netherlands in 2010, and start as favourites against the defending champions. Argentina are aiming to become only the third nation in history to win back-to-back world crowns, following on from Italy in the 1930s, and Brazil in 1958 and 1962, but will need to be at their very best against the Spaniards. The South Americans came from behind in the last 16 against Egypt and again against England in the semi-finals, with their fight and team spirit making up for weaknesses elsewhere

programme.title: The Full Monty
programme.desc: Six unemployed men from Sheffield decide to become strippers for one night only in a bid to raise extra money. However, with no dancing experience and far from perfect physiques, the men rely on the unique selling point of complete nudity to impress the punters. Comedy, starring Robert Carlyle, Mark Addy, Tom Wilkinson, Hugo Speer and Paul Barber

programme.title: There's a Gangster In My Flat - Panorama
programme.desc: Hazel Martin speaks to flat owners who paid reputable agencies to do tenancy checks but still ended up handing over their keys to criminals using fake IDs and references to take advantage of lax security in the rental market. Even when there appears to be clear evidence of criminal behaviour, gangs are escaping prosecution and landlords say it can take months to re-possess their properties

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Choirmaster Gareth Malone is hoping that the New Forest property market hits all the right notes, as he enlists the help of Sonali Shah to find him and his family a new home in the Hampshire countryside. With mum Sian along for assistance, Gareth has one big request - to find a property with enough space to fit a grand piano, and a beautifully converted former schoolhouse may just provide the answer

Police unpicked a web of calls and CCTV to find their moment of proof. Plus, the couple who became ill after eating poisoned curry. With one person dead, police found themselves hunting for a murderer

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: Ashley John-Baptiste introduces the case of a 1992 killing in Oxfordshire, in which a 91-year-old man was stabbed to dead in his home, and how fibre analysis was used to match the suspect's clothing. There's also a look at the work of forensic fire investigator Dan Matthews, who helped unover the truth about a devastating house fire which led to the deaths of six children

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: It's a big day at police training for 14 new recruits hoping to make it into the ranks of the Bermuda Police Service. Hopefuls hailing from across the globe have applied, including UK-born nurse Holly Lavigne-Smith, who is seeking to pursue a new career. Away from the front line, the British officer nicknamed Woody has struggled to find love during his eight years on the island, but today is welcoming his new girlfriend to Bermuda

programme.title: Rick Stein's Australia
programme.desc: Rick leaves Moree and travels through the traditional lands of the Gomeroi people, where he meets First Nations' pioneer Kerrie Saunders, who is leading a community initiative to reintroduce the use of native grains. He then visits the once-prosperous former port of Bourke, and joins Gary and Samantha Mooring, who are working to keep their 60,000-acre sheep station going in the face of changing tastes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps house hunter Andrew swap city life in Amsterdam for a characterful cottage in the Peak District for him and his faitful dog Edna. programme.desc: A portrait of Northern Irish dancer Melissa Hamilton, charting her late start, determination and rise to principal with the Royal Ballet against all expectations. programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: Documentary following teams of detectives and specialists involved in murder investigations. In the early hours of the morning in Droitwich Spa, Worcestershire, a 999 call is made by Mohamed Samak saying he has found his wife Jo in their home with a knife in her stomach. Despite attempts to resuscitate her, Jo is declared dead at the scene and the officers arrest the husband on suspicion of murder. But as the investigation continues, the overall picture is more suggestive of suicide rather than homicide

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: Police officers in Bermuda - including around 30 from the UK - deal with the highs and lows of policing this British Overseas Territory. Bermuda's natural beauty is renowned around the world, and nowhere illustrates this more than Cooper's Island Nature Reserve on the south-east tip of the island. Cameras record officers searching for lawbreakers in an ecological nature reserve and the coastguard conducting scuba-diving exercises that include recovering a fake dead body. Narrated by Shyko Amos

programme.desc: Atlantic bluefin tuna have returned to Northern Ireland's coast, inspiring Ballycastle anglers Davy Frayne and Jonny Thursby to establish a new fishery.
